segments.md:
# The Desktop

A Mac-style desktop, declared — windows, a dock, menus, and an embedded
app, the whole thing built from shapes, gradients, and constraints with
no image assets to load. It is the library's widest proof: dozens of
independent relationships — which window is front, how far the dock has
swelled, what the menu bar belongs to — all held true at once by the
runtime while you drag, resize, zoom, and click.

- **Windows** are their own layer, created at runtime by name. One
  `Window` class carries the chrome — a title bar you drag, edges and
  corners you resize (each with its resize cursor), a shadow that deepens
  when the window is front, and close / minimize / zoom controls — and
  subclasses carry the contents.
- **Files** browses this repository's own documentation, its column tree
  derived live from the docs model. **Viewer** renders any of those
  documents. **AppWindow** hosts a whole other Declare program as an
  embedded child — the dock's Calendar opens the real calendar app.
- The **dock** magnifies by layout, not paint: each icon's width is a
  constraint over its resting center, so neighbors displace and the
  plate stretches to hold them. Minimized windows park here as live
  thumbnails, riding the same swell and wearing their app's icon.
- The **menu bar** is whole menus expressed as records, and it follows
  focus — the menus belong to the front window's application, with a
  brand mark in the leading slot, a live Window menu, and a Help menu
  whose search field is a real text input. Keyboard shortcuts dispatch
  from the same records the bar is drawn from.

Every icon, control, and texture is drawn from shapes and gradients, so
the desktop carries no image payload at all — pure description. (The one
bitmap is a certain music-video still, and it earns its keep.)

segments.html:
// The window classes below are created only at runtime, by name —
// `app.createView("FilesWindow", …)` — never as a declared tag. Class
// reachability is otherwise a static fact (unused classes are dropped, which
// is what tree-shakes production builds), and a name inside a runtime string
// is invisible to that analysis. `use` is the explicit bridge: keep these
// classes registered for creation by name.

use [ FilesWindow, ViewerWindow, AppWindow, AboutWindow, JotWindow, BrandMark, HelpSearch, AppGlyph, CautionBadge ]

segments.md:
## Window — chrome once, contents per subclass

Every window on the desktop is one of these. `Window` carries all of the
chrome — the shadow, the resize edges and corners, and the shade / zoom /
minimize behaviors — and each subclass (Files, Viewer, AppWindow, About,
Jot) supplies only its contents. Geometry the class shares is named
once (`barH`, `minW`, `grab`, `corner`…) so no derived position below
carries a bare number.

Two motions drive most of the class. **Open and close** run `baseScale`
from the icon a window launched from up to full size and back, so it grows
out of its dock icon and shrinks into it. **Minimize** doesn't hide a
window — it parks it in the dock as a live thumbnail that rides the same
magnification swell as the icons, blended by `miniT` from a free window
(0) to a docked one (1).

segments.html:
class Window extends View [ width = 560, height = 400, clip = true,
    fill = { theme.surface },
    stroke = { stroke(1, theme.line) },

    // The front window floats on a deeper, softer shadow; an inactive one sits
    // closer to the desktop on a lighter, tighter one.

    inkHi: Color = #0000006B,                    // front-window shadow ink
    inkLo: Color = #00000047,                    // inactive-window shadow ink

    shadow = { app.frontWin == this ? shadow(0, 12, 26, this.inkHi) : shadow(0, 5, 14, this.inkLo) },

    title: string = "",
    active: boolean = { app.frontWin == this },

    // named geometry — every derived position below reads these, so the
    // numbers exist in exactly one place

    barH: number = 35,                           // title-bar height; content starts below it
    minW: number = 340,                          // resize floors
    minH: number = 120,
    grab: number = 8,                            // edge strip thickness (grabbable band)
    corner: number = 16,                         // corner strip size (wins over the edges)

    // a plain window (an About box) is close-to-dismiss only: no resize, no
    // shade, no zoom — the strips vanish and the yellow/green lights go inert

    plain: boolean = false,

    // custom-chrome windows (Jots) keep the base's behavior — drag
    // bookkeeping, activation, shade/zoom, resize strips — but drop the
    // standard title bar and grip art, and supply their own chrome on top

    chrome: boolean = true,

    // Open zoom: the window grows from the position it launched from — small at
    // the source, most of the way there within the first few frames, settled in
    // under a quarter second on a strong ease-out. bornX/bornY hold that origin
    // in window coordinates (-1 = no origin, so the window zooms gently from its
    // own center instead).

    bornX: number = -1,
    bornY: number = -1,
    bornFrom: View = null,                       // the view the open came from, for the return trip
    pivotX = { this.bornX >= 0 ? this.bornX : this.width / 2 },
    pivotY = { this.bornY >= 0 ? this.bornY : this.height / 2 },
    born: Animator [ attribute = baseScale, from = { classroot.bornX >= 0 ? 0.15 : 0.85 }, to = 1,
        duration = 230, motion = cubicOut, started = true ],

    // ── the geometry split that makes a minimized window a dock citizen ──
    // Everything imperative (drag, spawn, the zoom animators) writes the base
    // slots wx/wy/baseScale; the real x/y/scale are constraints blending base
    // geometry toward a dock slot the window computes for itself — the same
    // wave math the icons run, over the same live inputs. So a parked window
    // magnifies, displaces, and rides the dock's widening exactly as an icon
    // does: not a placed thumbnail but a member of the wave. `miniT` (the
    // blend, animated 0↔1) is the whole minimize journey.

    wx: number = 0,
    wy: number = 0,
    baseScale: number = 1,                       // the free-window scale — the zoom animators drive this
    dockSlot: number = -1,                       // ≥0 = my slot in the dock's minimized section
    miniT: number = 0,                           // the park blend: 0 = free, 1 = docked (animated — the journey)

    // My slot comes from the shared mini layout (app.miniLayout), left-anchored
    // like the icon row so a magnified thumbnail grows rightward and never
    // crosses the divider. x/w are the slot's live geometry; rw is its resting
    // width, so w/rw is my share of the magnification swell. A thumbnail keeps
    // its aspect, centers vertically in its cell, and rounds its corners at
    // thumbnail scale.

    slotW: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.dockSlot >= 0 && this.dockSlot < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.dockSlot].w : 48 },
    slotRestW: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.dockSlot >= 0 && this.dockSlot < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.dockSlot].rw : 48 },
    slotX: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.dockSlot >= 0 && this.dockSlot < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.dockSlot].x : 0 },
    parkScale: number = { this.slotW / Math.max(1, this.width) },
    waveMag: number = { this.slotW / Math.max(1, this.slotRestW) },

    // A thumbnail sits centered in the dock plate (with a small margin), not
    // bottom-anchored like the icons. The plate's center is 49.5px off the
    // screen bottom (a 5.5px gap plus the 44px half-plate), and the window's
    // visual center is placed to land there.
    //
    // It scales around the badge baseline — about 14px above the plate floor,
    // screen y = app.height - 19 — rather than its own center, so on rollover it
    // grows upward, away from the floor. At rest (waveMag 1) that baseline is
    // the plate center, so a resting thumbnail is still centered.

    parkY: number = { app.height - 19 - 30.5 * this.waveMag - this.height * this.parkScale / 2 - app.menuBarH },

    baseRadius: number = 10,                     // the window's normal corner rounding
    thumbRadius: number = 3.5,                   // …and its rounding at thumbnail scale, in dock pixels
    cornerRadius = { this.baseRadius + (this.thumbRadius * this.waveMag / Math.max(0.02, this.parkScale) - this.baseRadius) * this.miniT },

    x = { this.wx + (this.slotX - this.wx) * this.miniT },
    y = { this.wy + (this.parkY - this.wy) * this.miniT },
    scale = { this.baseScale + (this.parkScale - this.baseScale) * this.miniT },

    // Each journey stops the other before it starts, so a restore mid-park never
    // leaves two animators racing `miniT`; and each completion handler checks
    // that it actually arrived, so an interrupted journey never commits the
    // destination's state.

    miniGo: Animator [ attribute = miniT, to = 1, duration = 260, motion = cubicIn,
        onStop() { app.nudge() },                // no state — just a re-derive poke for seat checks
        ],
    miniBack: Animator [ attribute = miniT, to = 0, duration = 260, motion = cubicOut,
        onStop() { if (classroot.miniT <= 0.01) app.unparked(classroot) },
        ],

    // Close runs the open in reverse: the window shrinks back to its return
    // spot, recomputed at close time from the origin view (which may have
    // moved), falling back to the birth snapshot if that view is gone.

    closing: boolean = false,

    beginClose() {
        if (closing) return
        closing = true
        if (bornFrom != null) {
            let ox = bornFrom.width / 2
            let oy = bornFrom.height / 2
            let n = bornFrom
            let top = null
            while (n != null) { ox += n.x || 0; oy += n.y || 0; top = n; n = n.parent }
            // the walk reaching app proves the origin view is still attached;
            // its result is app coords — shift into this window's frame
            if (top == app) { bornX = ox - this.x; bornY = oy - this.y - app.menuBarH }
            }
        this.die.start()
        },

    die: Animator [ attribute = baseScale, to = 0.15, duration = 200, motion = cubicIn,
        onStop() { app.reallyClose(classroot) },
        ],

    // drag/resize bookkeeping (the captured-move pattern: root-space anchors)

    px0: number = 0,
    py0: number = 0,
    wx0: number = 0,
    wy0: number = 0,
    wh0: number = 0,
    dragging: boolean = false,
    sizeMode: string = "",                       // "" | "r" | "b" | "rb"

    // Shade (the Jot's collapse widget) and zoom each remember what they
    // displace so they can restore it. Minimize sends the window down into the
    // dock's minimized section — the same open/close curve, aimed at its tile —
    // and back out when the tile is clicked.

    shaded: boolean = false,
    zoomed: boolean = false,
    minimized: boolean = false,
    saveH: number = 0,
    saveX: number = 0,
    saveY: number = 0,
    saveW: number = 0,

    // minimize does not hide: the window parks at its dock tile, scaled to
    // fit — a live thumbnail (an embedded app inside keeps running; its DOM
    // island never unmounts, only transforms). The tile above it is the
    // click target, so the parked window can't be poked at 8% scale.

    // activating any real window dismisses open About boxes — they're modals
    // in spirit: clicking anywhere else lets them go (the red light works too)

    activate() { app.frontWin = this; this.raise(app.lowestMini()); if (!plain) app.dismissAbouts() },
    onMouseDown() { this.activate() },

    // subclass content attaches after the base's children, so any content that
    // reaches the window's edges would eat the resize strips' events — the
    // chrome re-promotes above everything (order is a slot). Subclasses that
    // override onInit call raiseChrome() themselves.

    raiseChrome() {
        this.edgeL.raise()
        this.edgeR.raise()
        this.edgeB.raise()
        this.cornerBL.raise()
        this.grip.raise()
        this.veil.raise()                        // topmost of all — a background window's first-click catcher
        },

    onInit() { this.raiseChrome() },

    // Shade collapses the window down to just its title bar; zoom fills the
    // desktop, leaving the dock's band clear at the bottom.

    shadeToggle() {
        if (shaded) { this.height = saveH; shaded = false }
        else { saveH = this.height; this.height = barH; shaded = true }
        },

    zoomToggle() {
        if (zoomed) { this.wx = saveX; this.wy = saveY; this.width = saveW; this.height = saveH; zoomed = false }
        else {
            saveX = this.wx; saveY = this.wy; saveW = this.width; saveH = this.height
            this.wx = 12; this.wy = 8
            this.width = app.width - 24
            this.height = app.height - 130            // 130 ≈ the dock band + margins
            zoomed = true
            }
        },

    wl0: number = 0,                             // window x at size start (left-edge modes)

    beginSize(mode) {
        if (plain) return
        this.activate()
        sizeMode = "" + mode
        px0 = app.pointerX
        py0 = app.pointerY
        wx0 = this.width
        wh0 = this.height
        wl0 = this.wx
        },

    // The grabbable set composes into "l" | "r" | "b" | "lb" | "rb". A left-edge
    // drag keeps the right edge fixed, so x follows the width.

    moveSize() {
        if (sizeMode == "") return
        const dx = app.pointerX - px0
        const dy = app.pointerY - py0
        if (sizeMode.indexOf("r") >= 0) this.width = Math.max(minW, wx0 + dx)
        if (sizeMode.indexOf("l") >= 0) {
            const w = Math.max(minW, wx0 - dx)
            this.wx = wl0 + (wx0 - w)
            this.width = w
            }
        if (sizeMode.indexOf("b") >= 0) this.height = Math.max(minH, wh0 + dy)
        },

    lightsHover: boolean = false,

    bar: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = { classroot.barH }, visible = { classroot.chrome },

        // The active window's bar carries a subtle vertical gradient; an
        // inactive one drops to the flat background tone.

        fill = { classroot.active
                ? (app.darkMode ? gradient(0x3B3B40, 0x2E2E32) : gradient(0xFAFAFC, 0xE6E6EA))
                : theme.bg },
        onMouseDown() {
            classroot.activate()
            classroot.dragging = true
            classroot.px0 = app.pointerX
            classroot.py0 = app.pointerY
            classroot.wx0 = classroot.wx
            classroot.wy0 = classroot.wy
            },
        onMouseMove() {
            if (!classroot.dragging) return
            // clamped so a window can never be dragged beyond reach: some of
            // it stays on the desktop on every side
            classroot.wx = Math.max(-200, Math.min(app.width - 60, classroot.wx0 + app.pointerX - classroot.px0))
            classroot.wy = Math.max(0, Math.min(app.height - 90, classroot.wy0 + app.pointerY - classroot.py0))
            },
        onMouseUp() { classroot.dragging = false },

        // The three window controls — close, minimize, zoom. Gray when the
        // window is inactive. Rollover is scoped to the controls block alone
        // (the bounding rectangle of the three circles), not the whole bar:
        // hovering it colorizes even an inactive window's controls and reveals
        // their glyphs, while the rest of the bar does nothing.
        //
        // 14px circles on a 20px pitch, so the block is 2·20 + 14 = 54 wide,
        // centered in the 35px bar.

        lights: View [ x = 13, y = 10.5, width = 54, height = 14,
            onMouseOver() { classroot.lightsHover = true },
            onMouseOut()  { classroot.lightsHover = false },
            close: TrafficLight [ x = 0, c = #EC6A5E, rim = #D25349, ink = #7B1006, sym = "close",
                on = { classroot.active || classroot.lightsHover }, showGlyph = { classroot.lightsHover },
                onMouseOver() { classroot.lightsHover = true },
                onMouseOut()  { classroot.lightsHover = false },
                onClick() { app.closeWin(classroot) },
                ],
            mini: TrafficLight [ x = 20, c = #F5BF4F, rim = #D9A340, ink = #8F5B00, sym = "min",
                on = { classroot.active || classroot.lightsHover }, showGlyph = { classroot.lightsHover },
                onMouseOver() { classroot.lightsHover = true },
                onMouseOut()  { classroot.lightsHover = false },
                onClick() { if (!classroot.plain) app.minimizeWin(classroot) },
                ],
            zoom: TrafficLight [ x = 40, c = #61C554, rim = #4CAF45, ink = #145E14, sym = "zoom",
                on = { classroot.active || classroot.lightsHover }, showGlyph = { classroot.lightsHover },
                onMouseOver() { classroot.lightsHover = true },
                onMouseOut()  { classroot.lightsHover = false },
                onClick() { if (!classroot.plain) classroot.zoomToggle() },
                ],
            ],

        cap: Text [ x = center, y = center, fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold,
            textColor = { classroot.active ? theme.text : theme.textMuted },
            text = { classroot.title } ],

        // the separator under the title bar — present active or not

        sep: View [ x = 0, y = { classroot.barH - 1 }, width = { parent.width }, height = 1, fill = { theme.line } ],
        ],

    // Resize strips on every edge and corner except the top (the title bar owns
    // that): the left and right edges, the bottom edge, and the two bottom
    // corners, each with its own resize cursor. Three cursor shapes — ↔ for a
    // vertical edge, ↕ for the bottom, and the diagonals for the corners. A
    // corner overlaps the ends of its edges and wins the hit-test.

    edgeL: View [ x = 0, y = { classroot.barH }, width = { classroot.grab }, visible = { !classroot.plain && !classroot.shaded },
        height = { parent.height - classroot.barH - classroot.corner }, cursor = "ew-resize",
        onMouseDown() { classroot.beginSize("l") },
        onMouseMove() { classroot.moveSize() },
        onMouseUp()   { classroot.sizeMode = "" },
        ],
    edgeR: View [ x = { parent.width - classroot.grab }, y = { classroot.barH }, width = { classroot.grab }, visible = { !classroot.plain && !classroot.shaded },
        height = { parent.height - classroot.barH - classroot.corner }, cursor = "ew-resize",
        onMouseDown() { classroot.beginSize("r") },
        onMouseMove() { classroot.moveSize() },
        onMouseUp()   { classroot.sizeMode = "" },
        ],
    edgeB: View [ x = { classroot.corner }, y = { parent.height - classroot.grab }, visible = { !classroot.plain && !classroot.shaded },
        width = { parent.width - 2 * classroot.corner }, height = { classroot.grab }, cursor = "ns-resize",
        onMouseDown() { classroot.beginSize("b") },
        onMouseMove() { classroot.moveSize() },
        onMouseUp()   { classroot.sizeMode = "" },
        ],
    cornerBL: View [ x = 0, y = { parent.height - classroot.corner }, visible = { !classroot.plain && !classroot.shaded },
        width = { classroot.corner }, height = { classroot.corner }, cursor = "nesw-resize",
        onMouseDown() { classroot.beginSize("lb") },
        onMouseMove() { classroot.moveSize() },
        onMouseUp()   { classroot.sizeMode = "" },
        ],

    // The bottom-right corner also draws a small grip affordance.

    grip: View [ x = { parent.width - classroot.corner }, y = { parent.height - classroot.corner }, visible = { !classroot.plain && !classroot.shaded },
        width = { classroot.corner }, height = { classroot.corner }, cursor = "nwse-resize",
        onMouseDown() { classroot.beginSize("rb") },
        onMouseMove() { classroot.moveSize() },
        onMouseUp()   { classroot.sizeMode = "" },
        draw(d) {
            if (!classroot.chrome) return            // custom chrome resizes here too, but unmarked
            d.strokeStyle = theme.textFaint
            d.lineWidth = 1
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo(6, 14); d.lineTo(14, 6)
            d.moveTo(10, 14); d.lineTo(14, 10)
            d.stroke()
            },
        ],

    // The first-click veil — a swappable policy (app.firstClickActivatesOnly).
    // A background window's content is covered by this transparent view, so a
    // click there raises the window and goes no further: the content (built-in
    // or an embedded app's island alike) never hears it, because the veil is the
    // hit target and events don't bubble. The chrome stays live — the title bar,
    // the controls, and the veil itself all activate. It sits topmost
    // (raiseChrome) and exists only while the window is in the background, so an
    // active window's content receives everything as usual.

    veil: View [ visible = { !classroot.active && app.firstClickActivatesOnly },
        x = 0, y = { classroot.barH },
        width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height - classroot.barH },
        onMouseDown() { classroot.activate() },
        ],
    ]

segments.md:
## TrafficLight — a window control

The three round window controls. Each is a filled circle with a hairline
rim a shade darker than the fill (never black), and on bar-hover it reveals
a glyph drawn in a dark tone of the same hue — a thick rounded ×, a minus
bar, and a pair of triangles split along the diagonal for zoom.

segments.html:
class TrafficLight extends View [ y = 0, width = 14, height = 14, cornerRadius = 7,
    c:   Color = #EC6A5E,
    rim: Color = #D25349,
    ink: Color = #7B1006,
    sym: string = "",                            // "close" | "min" | "zoom"
    on: boolean = false,                         // the window's active state, fed at the use site
    showGlyph: boolean = false,                  // bar-hover, fed at the use site

    // The rims are hairlines — a half-pixel stroke, hue-toned when lit and a
    // barely-there gray when not.

    fill = { on ? c : (app.darkMode ? 0x54545A : 0xD2D2D6) },
    faint: Color = #00000014,                    // the barely-there rim of an inactive control
    stroke = { stroke(0.5, on ? rim : this.faint) },

    // The glyph paths are authored in the control's own 14px box.

    g: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 14, height = 14,
        visible = { classroot.showGlyph && classroot.on },
        draw(d) {
            d.strokeStyle = classroot.ink
            d.fillStyle = classroot.ink
            d.lineCap = "round"
            if (classroot.sym == "close") {
                d.lineWidth = 1.6
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(4.5, 4.5); d.lineTo(9.5, 9.5)
                d.moveTo(9.5, 4.5); d.lineTo(4.5, 9.5)
                d.stroke()
                }
            else if (classroot.sym == "min") {
                d.lineWidth = 1.8
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(3.5, 7); d.lineTo(10.5, 7)
                d.stroke()
                }
            else if (classroot.sym == "zoom") {
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(4.1, 4.1); d.lineTo(8.5, 4.1); d.lineTo(4.1, 8.5)
                d.closePath(); d.fill()
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(9.9, 9.9); d.lineTo(5.5, 9.9); d.lineTo(9.9, 5.5)
                d.closePath(); d.fill()
                }
            },
        ],
    ]

segments.md:
## BrandMark — Declare's identity in the leading menu-bar slot

A white disc carrying Declare's `[ ]` brackets in black monospace. It reads
cleanly on any wallpaper and is a calm counterpart to the colored dock icons.

segments.html:
class BrandMark extends View [ width = 18, height = 18, cornerRadius = 9,
    fill = #FFFFFF,

    Text [ x = center, y = center, fontSize = 9, fontWeight = bold,
        textColor = #000000, text = "[ ]" ],
    ]

segments.md:
## AppGlyph — a standalone app icon at any size

The dock's rounded plate plus an illustration for a given kind or glyph. The
"Not implemented" alert reuses it so an app's mark is identical there and in
the dock — one renderer, `app.drawKindArt`.

segments.html:
class AppGlyph extends View [ width = { this.edge }, height = { this.edge },
    edge: number = 64,
    kind: string = "",
    glyph: string = "",
    gsize: number = 0.42,
    hue1: Color = #4A90D9,
    hue2: Color = #2E5FA3,
    cornerRadius = { this.edge * 0.23 }, clip = true,
    fill = { gradient(this.hue1, this.hue2) },

    g: Text [ x = center, y = center, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = #FFFFFF,
        fontSize = { classroot.edge * classroot.gsize },
        visible = { classroot.glyph != "" },
        text = { classroot.glyph } ],
    art: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height },
        visible = { classroot.kind != "" },
        draw(d) { d.scale(this.width / 48, this.width / 48); app.drawKindArt(d, classroot.kind, classroot.hue1) },
        ],
    ]

segments.md:
## CautionBadge — a warning mark

A rounded yellow triangle with a `!`, over a white halo so it reads against
any app icon behind it. Drawn in a 48-unit box.

segments.html:
class CautionBadge extends View [ width = { this.edge }, height = { this.edge },
    edge: number = 30,
    draw(d) {
        d.scale(this.edge / 48, this.edge / 48)
        const tri = (e, r) => {
            const P = [[24, 5 - e], [44 + e, 41 + e * 0.5], [4 - e, 41 + e * 0.5]]
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo((P[0][0] + P[2][0]) / 2, (P[0][1] + P[2][1]) / 2)
            d.arcTo(P[0][0], P[0][1], P[1][0], P[1][1], r)
            d.arcTo(P[1][0], P[1][1], P[2][0], P[2][1], r)
            d.arcTo(P[2][0], P[2][1], P[0][0], P[0][1], r)
            d.closePath()
            }
        d.fillStyle = 0xFFFFFF                        // the halo
        tri(2.6, 7); d.fill()
        const g = d.createLinearGradient(0, 3, 0, 43)
        g.addColorStop(0, "#FFD426"); g.addColorStop(1, "#F5B800")
        d.fillStyle = g
        tri(0, 6); d.fill()
        d.fillStyle = 0x3A2E00                        // the exclamation
        d.fillRect(22, 17, 4, 13)
        d.beginPath(); d.arc(24, 34, 2.3, 0, 6.3, false); d.fill()
        },
    ]

segments.md:
## HelpSearch — a real input inside a menu overlay

The Help menu's search field, rendered as a menu `kind:` row: the row's
chrome (no highlight, no pick) is the menu's, and inside sits the library
`SearchField` with a live `TextInput` — a genuine test of keyboard focus
inside a raised layer.

segments.html:
class HelpSearch extends View [ height = 40,
    width = { parent.width },

    // the field reaches to 12px from the menu's edges,
    // past the kind-row host's standard 24px inset — hence the negative x

    sf: SearchField [ x = -12, y = 5, width = { parent.width + 10 }, height = 30 ],

## AboutWindow — a tall, image-topped About box

One class serves the desktop and every application: `forApp` picks the art,
the name, and the blurb. `plain` windows give up resize and shade/zoom — the
close control is the whole interface.

segments.html:
class AboutWindow extends Window [ width = 280, height = 380, plain = true,
    forApp: string = "desktop",                  // desktop | files | markdown | calendar | declareviewer | jots

    aname: string = { this.forApp == "files" ? "Files"
        : this.forApp == "markdown" ? "Markdown"
        : this.forApp == "calendar" ? "Declare Calendar"
        : this.forApp == "declareviewer" ? "Declare Viewer"
        : this.forApp == "jots" ? "Jots" : "Declare Desktop" },

    blurb: string = { this.forApp == "files" ? "Browses the live documentation model — every chapter, every element."
        : this.forApp == "markdown" ? "Renders the real files, and reference pages synthesized from the model."
        : this.forApp == "declareviewer" ? "Reader, source, and live editor over any Declare program — here, hosted as an embedded app."
        : this.forApp == "calendar" ? "The actual apps/calendar, running as an embedded child app in this window's island."
        : this.forApp == "jots" ? "Small paper notes with their own window chrome — built into this program."
        : "A Mac desktop, declared. Windows, dock, and menus are views and records, all drawn — no bitmaps, no borrowed art." },

    body: View [ x = 0, y = { classroot.barH }, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height - classroot.barH },
        col: View [ x = 24, y = 22, width = { parent.width - 48 },
            layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 10 ],

            art: View [ x = center, width = 96, height = 96, cornerRadius = 22, clip = true,
                fill = { classroot.forApp == "markdown" ? gradient(0x8E7CE8, 0x5A46B8)
                    : classroot.forApp == "files" ? gradient(0x59B0F6, 0x1D6FD1)
                    : classroot.forApp == "calendar" ? gradient(0xFFFFFF, 0xEFEFF2)
                    : classroot.forApp == "declareviewer" ? gradient(0x2E6FE0, 0x12A594)
                    : classroot.forApp == "jots" ? gradient(0xFCF4A7, 0xF3E27E)
                    : gradient(0x4AA8E8, 0x35C4B5) },

                // per-app art, authored in a 48-box and doubled (the dock's rule)

                a: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 48, height = 48, scale = 2,
                    draw(d) {
                        const k = classroot.forApp
                        const rr = (x, y, w, h, r) => {
                            d.moveTo(x + r, y)
                            d.lineTo(x + w - r, y); d.arc(x + w - r, y + r, r, -Math.PI / 2, 0, false)
                            d.lineTo(x + w, y + h - r); d.arc(x + w - r, y + h - r, r, 0, Math.PI / 2, false)
                            d.lineTo(x + r, y + h); d.arc(x + r, y + h - r, r, Math.PI / 2, Math.PI, false)
                            d.lineTo(x, y + r); d.arc(x + r, y + r, r, Math.PI, Math.PI * 1.5, false)
                            d.closePath()
                            }
                        if (k == "calendar") {
                            d.fillStyle = 0xFB4238
                            d.fillRect(0, 0, 48, 13)
                            }
                        else if (k == "files") {
                            // the folder — the dock icon's art, one rendition
                            d.fillStyle = 0xD9E7FA
                            d.beginPath()
                            rr(9, 13.5, 14, 8, 2)
                            d.fill()
                            d.beginPath()
                            rr(9, 16, 30, 19, 2.5)
                            d.fill()
                            d.fillStyle = 0xFFFFFF
                            d.beginPath()
                            rr(9, 20, 30, 15, 2.5)
                            d.fill()
                            }
                        else if (k == "jots") {
                            d.fillStyle = 0xF3DC49
                            d.fillRect(0, 0, 48, 10)
                            d.strokeStyle = 0xE0CE66
                            d.lineWidth = 2
                            d.beginPath()
                            d.moveTo(8, 19); d.lineTo(40, 19)
                            d.moveTo(8, 27); d.lineTo(40, 27)
                            d.moveTo(8, 35); d.lineTo(26, 35)
                            d.stroke()
                            }
                        },
                    ],
                t: Text [ x = center, y = center, fontSize = 34, fontWeight = bold, textColor = 0xFFFFFF,
                    visible = { classroot.forApp == "markdown" || classroot.forApp == "desktop" || classroot.forApp == "declareviewer" },
                    text = { classroot.forApp == "markdown" ? "M↓" : "[  ]" } ],
                cwk: Text [ x = center, y = 4, fontWeight = bold, textColor = 0xFFFFFF, fontSize = 15,
                    visible = { classroot.forApp == "calendar" }, text = { app.dayName } ],
                cnum: Text [ x = center, y = 30, fontSize = 46, textColor = 0x1D1D1F,
                    visible = { classroot.forApp == "calendar" }, text = { "" + app.dayNum } ],
                ],

            nm: Text [ x = center, fontSize = 17, fontWeight = bold, textColor = { theme.text },
                text = { classroot.aname } ],
            ver: Text [ x = center, fontSize = 11, textColor = { theme.textMuted },
                text = "Version pre-1.0" ],
            gap: View [ width = 4, height = 2 ],
            bl: Text [ fontSize = 11, wrap = true, width = { parent.width }, textAlign = center,
                textColor = { theme.textMuted }, text = { classroot.blurb } ],
            gap2: View [ width = 4, height = 8 ],
            cp: Text [ x = center, fontSize = 10, textColor = { theme.textFaint },
                text = "Built in Declare · MIT · © 2026 David Temkin" ],
            ],
        ],
    ]

## FolderIcon / FileIcon — small drawn file-browser icons

A blue folder and a dog-eared page, drawn from rounded rectangles and a few
strokes. (The .5 offsets put 1px strokes on pixel centers, for crisp
hairlines.)

segments.html:
class FolderIcon extends View [ width = 17, height = 13,
    draw(d) {
        const rr = (x, y, w, h, r) => {
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo(x + r, y)
            d.lineTo(x + w - r, y); d.arc(x + w - r, y + r, r, -Math.PI / 2, 0, false)
            d.lineTo(x + w, y + h - r); d.arc(x + w - r, y + h - r, r, 0, Math.PI / 2, false)
            d.lineTo(x + r, y + h); d.arc(x + r, y + h - r, r, Math.PI / 2, Math.PI, false)
            d.lineTo(x, y + r); d.arc(x + r, y + r, r, Math.PI, Math.PI * 1.5, false)
            d.closePath()
            }
        d.fillStyle = 0x4E9FEA
        rr(0, 0, 7.5, 4, 1.5); d.fill()              // the back tab
        d.fillStyle = 0x6AB2F5
        rr(0, 1.8, 17, 11, 1.5); d.fill()            // the body
        d.fillStyle = 0x8CC4F8
        rr(0, 1.8, 17, 3.2, 1.5); d.fill()           // the top lip highlight
        },
    ]


class FileIcon extends View [ width = 13, height = 16,
    draw(d) {
        d.fillStyle = 0xFFFFFF
        d.strokeStyle = 0xB4B4BA
        d.lineWidth = 1
        d.beginPath()
        d.moveTo(0.5, 0.5); d.lineTo(8.5, 0.5); d.lineTo(12.5, 4.5)
        d.lineTo(12.5, 15.5); d.lineTo(0.5, 15.5)
        d.closePath(); d.fill(); d.stroke()
        d.fillStyle = 0xDDDDE2                       // the dog-ear fold
        d.beginPath()
        d.moveTo(8.5, 0.5); d.lineTo(8.5, 4.5); d.lineTo(12.5, 4.5)
        d.closePath(); d.fill(); d.stroke()
        },
    ]

segments.md:
## FilesWindow — a column browser over the repo's own documentation

Three columns — folders, files, and a preview — over a tree derived live from
the documentation model (`app.docsModel`), so nothing here goes stale when the
docs move. The window's title is the deepest open folder. The columns scroll
and clip independently, the dividers between them drag, and a double-click
opens a document in the Viewer.

segments.html:
class FilesWindow extends Window [

    // the SELECTION CHAIN: the open nodes' names, newline-joined, one per column
    // ("" = nothing open). Every level of the hierarchy — even the top — is DATA
    // (app.docsModel.value.browse, the one documentation model); this window
    // chooses no categories of its own.

    selPath: string = "",
    colW: number = 230,                          // one column's width (drag any seam to resize all)
    rowH: number = 26,                           // one row
    cw0: number = 0,                             // divider-drag anchors
    cpx0: number = 0,

    // the window's name is the deepest open node, or "Documentation"

    title = { this.selPath != "" ? (("" + this.selPath).split("\n").pop() ?? "Documentation") : "Documentation" },

    // Walk `browse` along selPath into a flat list of columns: one LIST column
    // per level (its child nodes), then a DETAIL column once the deepest
    // selection is a leaf. Each item carries `at` — the exact selPath that opens
    // it — so a row's click is one assignment and truncation is automatic (the
    // list re-derives). Miller columns fall straight out of a recursive walk.

    columnData(selPath, model) {
        const parts = selPath == "" ? [] : selPath.split("\n")
        const item = (n, p) => ({ name: "" + n.name, kind: "" + (n.kind || ""), label: "" + (n.label || ""),
            hasKids: (n.children != null && n.children.length > 0),
            path: "" + (n.path || ""), doc: "" + (n.doc || ""), preview: "" + (n.preview || ""),
            at: p.join("\n") })
        const cols = []
        let level = ((model || ({ })).browse) || []
        cols.push(({ items: level.map((n) => item(n, [n.name])) }))
        for (let k = 0; k < parts.length; k = k + 1) {
            const chosen = level.find((n) => n.name == parts[k])
            if (chosen == null) break
            const prefix = parts.slice(0, k + 1)
            if (chosen.children != null && chosen.children.length > 0) {
                level = chosen.children
                cols.push(({ items: level.map((n) => item(n, prefix.concat([n.name]))) }))
            } else {
                cols.push(({ leaf: item(chosen, prefix) }))
                break
            }
        }
        return cols
        },
    cols: Dataset [ contents = { ({ list: classroot.columnData(classroot.selPath, app.docsModel.value) }) } ],

    // open a leaf — every leaf is a document. An authored file (`path`) opens its
    // source; a hydrated page (`doc`, assembled by the model) opens whole. Same
    // Markdown window either way; the browser special-cases nothing.

    openLeaf(leaf) {
        const d = "" + (leaf.doc || ""), p = "" + (leaf.path || "")
        if (d != "") { app.openDoc(d, "" + leaf.name, this); return }
        if (p != "") app.openViewer("../../" + p, "" + leaf.name, this)
        },

    body: View [ x = 0, y = { classroot.barH }, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height - classroot.barH },
        fill = { theme.bg },

        // the column STRIP: a horizontal row as wide as the open columns need,
        // inside a viewport that scrolls RIGHT (scrollsX) as they accumulate — a
        // trackpad's horizontal delta drives it, and the runtime swallows that
        // gesture so it never becomes Safari's back-swipe (dom-backend setScrollX).

        strip: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height }, scrollsX = true,
            datapath = { classroot.cols.value },
            row: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { (classroot.cols.value.list || []).length * classroot.colW }, height = { parent.height },
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 0 ],

                // ONE column per browse level (its child nodes), then the final
                // leaf-DETAIL column once the deepest selection has no children.

                View [ datapath = :list[], width = { classroot.colW }, height = { parent.height }, clip = true, fill = { theme.bg },

                    // a freshly-opened column reveals itself — the strip scrolls
                    // right so drilling always brings the new column into view

                    onInit() { setTimeout(() => this.scrollIntoView("nearest"), 0) },

                    // a LIST column — this level's nodes as selectable rows

                    lst: View [ visible = { :items != null }, x = 0, y = 0, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height }, scrolls = true,
                        inner: View [ x = 0, y = 6, width = { parent.width }, height = { this.contentHeight + 12 },
                            layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 1 ],
                            View [ datapath = :items[], width = { parent.width }, height = { classroot.rowH },
                                on: boolean = { classroot.selPath != "" && (("" + classroot.selPath) + "\n").indexOf(("" + :at) + "\n") == 0 },
                                onClick() { classroot.activate(); classroot.selPath = "" + :at },
                                onDblClick() { if (!:hasKids) classroot.openLeaf(({ name: "" + :name, path: "" + :path, doc: "" + :doc })) },
                                hl: View [ x = 4, y = 0, width = { parent.width - 8 }, height = { classroot.rowH }, cornerRadius = 5,
                                    visible = { parent.on }, fill = { theme.accent } ],
                                fo: FolderIcon [ x = 11, y = center, visible = { :hasKids } ],
                                fi: FileIcon [ x = 12, y = center, visible = { !:hasKids } ],
                                nm: Text [ x = 34, y = center, fontSize = 13, wrap = false, width = { parent.width - 52 },
                                    textColor = { parent.on ? theme.accentText : theme.text }, text = { "" + :name } ],
                                chev: Text [ x = { parent.width - 15 }, y = center, fontSize = 12, visible = { :hasKids },
                                    textColor = { parent.on ? theme.accentText : theme.textFaint }, text = "›" ],
                                ],
                            ],
                        edge: View [ x = { parent.width - 1 }, y = 0, width = 1, height = { parent.height }, fill = { theme.line } ],
                        ],

                    // the LEAF DETAIL column — name, kind, a preview, and Open

                    det: View [ visible = { :leaf != null }, x = 18, y = 20, width = { parent.width - 36 },
                        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 11 ],
                        dn: Text [ fontSize = 14, fontWeight = semibold, wrap = true, width = { parent.width },
                            textColor = { theme.text }, text = { "" + :leaf.name } ],
                        dk: Text [ fontSize = 11, wrap = false, textColor = { theme.textFaint },
                            text = { "" + :leaf.label } ],
                        dp: Text [ fontSize = 12, wrap = true, width = { parent.width },
                            textColor = { theme.text }, text = { "" + :leaf.preview } ],
                        Button [ width = 92, label = "Open", primary = true, visible = { :leaf.path != "" || :leaf.doc != "" },
                            onClick() { classroot.openLeaf(:leaf) },
                            ],
                        ],

                    // drag any seam to resize every column (uniform Finder-style width)

                    grip: View [ x = { parent.width - 4 }, y = 0, width = 8, height = { parent.height }, cursor = "col-resize",
                        onMouseDown() { classroot.activate(); classroot.cw0 = classroot.colW; classroot.cpx0 = app.pointerX },
                        onMouseMove() { if (classroot.cw0 > 0) classroot.colW = Math.max(160, Math.min(420, classroot.cw0 + app.pointerX - classroot.cpx0)) },
                        onMouseUp() { classroot.cw0 = 0 },
                        ],
                    ],
                ],
            ],
        ],
    ]

segments.md:
## AppWindow — a whole Declare app inside a desktop window

The window frame around an `AppIsland` — the library's app-embedding primitive.
Give the island a `program` and it mounts that app as its own child: its own
reactive graph, input router, sized to the island's box. The path is relative,
so `"../../calendar/calendar"` reaches the real calendar app: no copy, no shim.
The window adds only what a window owns — the title (taken from the running
child's own name, `island.childName`), activation, and the `dark` + `extraEnv`
environment it hands the island.

segments.html:
class AppWindow extends Window [ width = 880, height = 560,
    appPath: string = "",
    extraEnv: string = "",                       // appended env parameters ("program=…")

    // the window's name is the HOSTED app's name, reflected up from the embedded
    // child (`island.childName`): the viewer titles by the file it shows and
    // follows its navigation. `appTitle` is the fallback until the child is up.

    appTitle: string = "",
    title = { this.island.childName != "" ? this.island.childName : this.appTitle },

    // the slot's ENV segment (after `|`) is the embedding environment — the
    // host parses it into the child's reactive `app.env`, at mount and on
    // every change: toggling the desktop's appearance re-marks the slot and
    // the hosted app re-derives (its Auto mode follows `app.env.dark`)

    // a press anywhere in the hosted app also lands here (the router remaps
    // embedded presses to the island — dom-backend), so clicking the child's
    // content raises this window exactly as clicking any window body does

    island: AppIsland [ x = 0, y = { classroot.barH }, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height - classroot.barH },
        onMouseDown() { classroot.activate() },
        program = { classroot.appPath },
        env = { "dark=" + (app.darkMode ? "1" : "0")
            + (classroot.extraEnv != "" ? "&" + classroot.extraEnv : "") },
        ],
    ]

segments.md:
## ViewerWindow — a Markdown window over a real document

Renders a document with the library's `Markdown` view. A `"ref:"` path builds a
reference page from the documentation model; anything else fetches the real
file as text. Its title is the file's basename.

segments.html:
class ViewerWindow extends Window [ width = 640, height = 460,
    path: string = "",

    // the window's name is the FILE being viewed — its basename, not the path
    // (a "ref:" page names the element). The same basename rule the standalone
    // viewer app titles itself by, so the two agree.

    // a viewer opens either a real FILE (a `path` it fetches) or a HYDRATED doc
    // handed over whole (`inlineDoc` — a page the model already assembled, e.g.
    // an element's reference). `docName` titles a hydrated doc; a file titles by
    // its basename.

    inlineDoc: string = "",
    docName: string = "",
    title = { this.inlineDoc != "" ? this.docName : (this.path.split("/").pop() || "") },

    inline: string = { classroot.inlineDoc },

    src: DataSource [ format = "text", url = { classroot.path } ],

    // the scrolling body is a real pointer target covering the whole window,
    // and subclass children attach after the base's — so the Window's resize
    // strips re-promote above it (order is a slot) or edge drags never land

    onInit() {
        if (this.inline == "") this.src.fetch()
        this.raiseChrome()
        },

    body: View [ x = 0, y = { classroot.barH }, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height - classroot.barH },
        scrolls = true, selectable = true,

        // Reading margins: 24 at the sides, 18 above — and the pad's explicit
        // height adds the same 18 below the document, so the scroll extent ends
        // with breathing room instead of flush text.

        pad: View [ x = 24, y = 18, width = { parent.width - 48 },
            height = { this.doc.height + 18 },
            loading: Text [ fontSize = 13, textColor = { theme.textFaint },
                visible = { classroot.inline == "" && classroot.src.loading }, text = "Loading…" ],
            failed: Text [ fontSize = 13, textColor = { theme.textFaint }, wrap = true, width = { parent.width },
                visible = { classroot.inline == "" && classroot.src.failed },
                text = { "Could not load — " + (classroot.src.error || "") } ],
            doc: Markdown [ width = { parent.width },
                visible = { classroot.inline != "" || classroot.src.loaded },
                dark = { app.darkMode },
                text = { classroot.inline != "" ? classroot.inline : (classroot.src.value || "") } ],
            ],
        ],
    ]

segments.md:
## JotWindow — a sticky note with its own chrome

A custom window type: square corners, yellow paper, and — when active — a
saturated title strip carrying olive-outlined pale widgets (a close box at the
left; a zoom triangle and a collapse box at the right). An inactive note is
chromeless: the strip and widgets vanish into the paper. It keeps all of the
base `Window`'s behavior — drag, the activation shadow, shade, resize edges —
through `chrome = false`, so here chrome is a skin, not the skeleton.

segments.html:
class JotWindow extends Window [ width = 300, height = 250, chrome = false,
    baseRadius = 0, barH = 12, minW = 160, minH = 90,
    title = "",                                  // untitled — labels fall back to the app's name
    note: string = "",                           // the note's seed text

    // the note's palette: paper #FEF49C, active strip #FEEA3D, widget
    // linework #DBC501 dark olive over #FFF49C pale, and a half-pixel
    // #4E4E52 rim — a full CSS pixel reads as drawn-on lines, not an edge

    fill = #FEF49C,
    stroke = { stroke(0.5, 0x4E4E52) },
    inkHi = #00000052,
    inkLo = #00000038,
    shadow = { app.frontWin == this ? shadow(0, 10, 26, this.inkHi) : shadow(0, 3, 9, this.inkLo) },

    strip: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { parent.width }, height = { classroot.barH },
        fill = { classroot.active ? 0xFEEA3D : 0xFEF49C },
        onMouseDown() {
            classroot.activate()
            classroot.dragging = true
            classroot.px0 = app.pointerX
            classroot.py0 = app.pointerY
            classroot.wx0 = classroot.wx
            classroot.wy0 = classroot.wy
            },
        onMouseMove() {
            if (!classroot.dragging) return
            classroot.wx = Math.max(-200, Math.min(app.width - 60, classroot.wx0 + app.pointerX - classroot.px0))
            classroot.wy = Math.max(0, Math.min(app.height - 90, classroot.wy0 + app.pointerY - classroot.py0))
            },
        onMouseUp() { classroot.dragging = false },

        // the strip's widgets: dark olive outlines around pale interiors —
        // the close box 11px in from the left, the zoom triangle (right
        // angle at bottom-right) and the collapse box in from the right

        closeBox: View [ visible = { classroot.active }, x = 11, y = 2.25, width = 7.5, height = 7.5,
            fill = #FFF49C, stroke = { stroke(1.4, 0xDBC501) },
            onClick() { app.closeWin(classroot) },
            ],
        zoomW: View [ visible = { classroot.active }, x = { parent.width - 33 }, y = 2.25, width = 8, height = 8,
            onClick() { classroot.zoomToggle() },
            draw(d) {
                d.fillStyle = 0xFFF49C
                d.strokeStyle = 0xDBC501
                d.lineWidth = 1.4
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(0.7, 7.3); d.lineTo(7.3, 7.3); d.lineTo(7.3, 0.7)
                d.closePath(); d.fill(); d.stroke()
                },
            ],
        collapseW: View [ visible = { classroot.active }, x = { parent.width - 21 }, y = 2.25, width = 8, height = 8,
            onClick() { classroot.shadeToggle() },   // collapse down to just the strip
            draw(d) {
                d.fillStyle = 0xFFF49C
                d.fillRect(0.7, 0.7, 6.6, 6.6)
                d.strokeStyle = 0xDBC501
                d.lineWidth = 1.4
                d.beginPath()
                d.rect(0.7, 0.7, 6.6, 6.6)
                d.stroke()
                d.beginPath()
                d.moveTo(0.7, 3.1); d.lineTo(7.3, 3.1)
                d.stroke()
                },
            ],
        ],

    // the note body is formatted — a Markdown field on the paper (no input
    // chrome, no focus ring; the note's text is authored, not typed)

    pad: View [ x = 12, y = { classroot.barH + 6 }, clip = true,
        width = { parent.width - 24 }, height = { parent.height - classroot.barH - 16 },
        md: Markdown [ width = { parent.width }, dark = false,
            bodyColor = 0x1D1D1F, scale = 0.82, lineHeight = 1.45,
            text = { classroot.note } ],
        ],
    ]

segments.md:
## MiniTile — a minimized window's dock tile

An invisible click target that rides the same magnification swell as the
icons, so its hit area and label track the live thumbnail beneath it (the
thumbnail is the parked window itself). The tile sits in the dock layer above
the parked window, so a click restores it instead of poking its tiny content.
Its pill is the `DockIcon`'s, showing the window's title.

segments.html:
class MiniTile extends View [

    // a static pool member (no replication, no Dataset): `ix` is assigned at
    // the use site, and every property below is a pure constraint over the
    // app's mini state — one atomic write to miniRecs re-derives the whole
    // tile, so no per-row machinery exists to go stale

    ix: number = 0,
    on: boolean = { app.miniRecs != null && this.ix < app.miniRecs.length },
    visible = { this.on },
    lbl: string = { this.on ? "" + app.miniRecs[this.ix].label : "" },
    kind: string = { this.on ? "" + app.miniRecs[this.ix].kind : "" },
    hovered: boolean = false,

    // geometry straight from the mini layout (the same entries the parked
    // window reads), translated into the minis section's local space

    slotW: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.ix < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.ix].w : 48 },
    slotH: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.ix < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.ix].h : 48 },
    slotRestW: number = { app.miniLayout != null && this.ix < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.ix].rw : 48 },
    waveMag: number = { this.slotW / Math.max(1, this.slotRestW) },
    x = { (app.miniLayout != null && this.ix < app.miniLayout.length ? app.miniLayout[this.ix].x : 0) - (app.dock.x + app.dock.row.width + 32) },
    width = { this.slotW },
    height = { this.slotH },   // the thumbnail's aspect height (not icon-square), so the badge lands on the thumbnail's own corner
    y = { parent.height - 13.5 - 30.5 * this.waveMag - this.height / 2 },   // scales around the badge baseline (grows upward on rollover); at rest this is the plate center

    onMouseOver() { hovered = true },
    onMouseOut()  { hovered = false },
    onClick() { app.restoreMiniAt(this.ix) },

    // the app-icon badge — on the tile (the dock layer, above the plate's
    // frost, so it renders crisp), gated by geometry: it shows exactly while
    // the window is seated in this slot — its visual width equals the slot's
    // (both sides derive from the same layout entry, so the equality holds
    // through the magnification wave, and never mid-journey: a traveling or
    // restored window's scale·width can't match). Pure derived fact — no
    // arrival bookkeeping at all.

    seated: boolean = { app.seatCheck(app.miniWins, this.ix, this.slotW, app.winSeq, app.pointerX, app.dock.hot) },

    // The badge is the app's own dock icon at badge scale — same class, same
    // art, so the calendar badge carries the live date.
    // Its wave/size/position constraints are displaced at this use site, and
    // its pointer handlers forward to the tile (so no second pill appears).

    badge: DockIcon [
        visible = { classroot.seated },
        width = { app.badgeEdge * classroot.waveMag }, height = { app.badgeEdge * classroot.waveMag },

        // x tracks the thumbnail's right edge (2px inside it); y sits on the
        // dock's common badge baseline (13.5px above the plate floor), not the
        // thumbnail's own bottom — so a short or landscape thumbnail's badge
        // hangs below it while a tall one stays contained. The tile's center is
        // the plate center; baseline = center + (44 - 13.5).

        x = { parent.width - (app.badgeEdge + 2) * classroot.waveMag },
        y = { parent.height / 2 + (30.5 - app.badgeEdge) * classroot.waveMag },
        kind = { classroot.kind == "calendar" ? "calendar" : classroot.kind == "jots" ? "jots" : classroot.kind == "files" ? "files" : "" },
        glyph = { classroot.kind == "markdown" ? "M↓" : classroot.kind == "declareviewer" ? "[  ]" : "" },
        gsize = { classroot.kind == "declareviewer" ? 0.42 : 0.42 },
        hue1 = { classroot.kind == "markdown" ? 0x8E7CE8
            : classroot.kind == "declareviewer" ? 0x2E6FE0
            : classroot.kind == "calendar" ? 0xFFFFFF
            : classroot.kind == "jots" ? 0xFCF4A7 : 0x59B0F6 },
        hue2 = { classroot.kind == "markdown" ? 0x5A46B8
            : classroot.kind == "declareviewer" ? 0x12A594
            : classroot.kind == "calendar" ? 0xEFEFF2
            : classroot.kind == "jots" ? 0xF3E27E : 0x1D6FD1 },
        onMouseOver() { classroot.hovered = true },
        onMouseOut()  { classroot.hovered = false },
        onClick() { app.restoreMiniAt(classroot.ix) },
        ],

    // the label pill — the DockIcon's, verbatim geometry: it floats just above
    // the tile's OWN top (local y), tail tip meeting it, so it rides the
    // magnified crest exactly as the app-icon pill does

    pill: View [ visible = { hovered },
        width = { this.t.width + 26 }, height = 33,
        x = { (parent.width - this.width) / 2 },
        y = { parent.height / 2 - 102 },   // pinned to the shared pill baseline (~62px above the plate top), same Y as the app-icon pills
        bodyH: number = 22,
        draw(d) { app.drawPill(d, this.width, this.bodyH, app.darkMode) },
        t: Text [ x = center, y = { (this.parent.bodyH - this.height) / 2 }, fontSize = 13,
            textColor = { app.darkMode ? 0xF2F2F5 : 0x1D1D1F },
            text = { classroot ? "" + classroot.lbl : "" } ],
        ],
    ]

segments.md:
## DockIcon — a drawn, labeled icon that magnifies by layout

Each icon's live width springs toward a target read from the pointer's distance
to its resting center, so hovering the dock swells the icons under the pointer
and displaces their neighbors. The magnification is layout, not paint: the
widths really change and the plate stretches to hold them.

segments.html:
class DockIcon extends View [

    // per-icon inputs, fed at the use site

    ix: number = 0,                              // resting slot index
    slots: number = 9,                           // total icons — keep in step with the dock's row
    name: string = "",                           // the label pill's text
    hue1: Color = #4A90D9,                       // plate gradient, top → bottom
    hue2: Color = #2E5FA3,
    glyph: string = "",                          // a text glyph icon (⌘, ♪, …) …
    gsize: number = 0.42,                        //   … at this fraction of the plate (wide marks set less)
    kind: string = "",                           // … or drawn art: calendar | notes | mail | terminal | pencil | jots
    running: boolean = false,
    hovered: boolean = false,

    // shared geometry — one place for the numbers the constraints below share

    rest: number = { app.iconEdge },             // resting icon size (the shared cell)
    pitch: number = { app.iconEdge + 16 },       // slot-center spacing: the resting size plus a 16px gap
    strip: number = 16,                          // the running-dot band below the icons

    // the face is rendered ONCE at this fixed reference size and paint-scaled to
    // the live magnified width (the plate's `scale` below). It must be ≥ the
    // largest magnified size (rest · 1.55 ≈ 87pt) so the transform only ever
    // DOWNsamples the hi-res raster — smooth, no shimmer — while the illustration
    // and rim draw()s invalidate only on real content change (calendar day,
    // theme), never per magnification frame. This keeps the wave inside Safari's
    // frame budget: 9 GPU blits, not 9 vector re-rasters, every frame.

    face: number = 96,

    // the resting slot center in app coords — the magnification's source must
    // not read the magnified layout (a feedback loop); resting geometry is
    // fixed: `pitch` about the app's center

    cx0: number = { app.width / 2 + (this.ix - (this.slots - 1) / 2) * this.pitch - app.miniSpan / 2 },

    // magnification: the shared wave (app.dockMag) about my resting center,
    // read off the SMOOTHED pointer (dockX) so it glides every frame, and
    // scaled by the enter/exit envelope (the dock's `hot`) so entering and
    // leaving glide too. Neighbors displace as the widths change — the wave
    // is layout, not paint.

    // Each icon springs its OWN magnification toward a target read from the raw
    // pointer, so every icon is always mid-flight during motion and the swell
    // glides continuously — smoother than a single shared spring, which would
    // settle between the browser's coalesced pointer events and step. The
    // resting center (cx0) feeds the distance, so the swell never reads its own
    // magnified output.

    magTarget: number = { 1 + (app.dockMag(app.pointerX - this.cx0) - 1) * this.parent.parent.hotGate },
    mag: number = 1,
    magSpring: Spring [ attribute = mag, to = { classroot.magTarget }, stiffness = 1300, damping = 62, epsilon = 0.002 ],
    width  = { this.rest * this.mag },
    height = { this.width },
    y = { parent.height - this.height - this.strip - this.hopY },   // bottom-anchored above the dot strip

    // The launch bounce: a ballistic arc — decelerating up, gravity down —
    // twice, about 560ms per bounce, poked by the app when an application first
    // opens (or first opens again after a Quit). The group cascades `attribute`
    // to its members; each leg is one half of the parabola.

    hopY: number = 0,

    bounce: AnimatorGroup [ attribute = hopY, process = sequential,
        Animator [ to = 30, duration = 300, motion = quadOut ],
        Animator [ to = 0, duration = 260, motion = quadIn ],
        Animator [ to = 30, duration = 300, motion = quadOut ],
        Animator [ to = 0, duration = 260, motion = quadIn ],
        ],

    poke() { this.bounce.start() },

    onMouseOver() { hovered = true },
    onMouseOut()  { hovered = false },

    plate: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { classroot.face }, height = { classroot.face },
        // Paint-only magnification: the face is drawn once at `face`px and the
        // GPU scales it (about the top-left, so it fills the bottom-anchored box)
        // to the live width — no per-frame re-raster of the illustration or rim.
        // The DockIcon's LAYOUT width still grows, so neighbors displace as
        // before: the swell stays layout; only each icon's paint is cached.

        scale = { classroot.width / classroot.face },
        cornerRadius = { this.width * 0.23 }, clip = true,
        fill = { gradient(this.parent.hue1, this.parent.hue2) },

        // Glyph sizes stay fractional — rounding them would tick the art in
        // whole-pixel steps while the plate glides subpixel, a visible jitter.

        g: Text [ x = center, y = center, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = 0xFFFFFF,
            fontSize = { this.parent.width * classroot.gsize },
            visible = { classroot.glyph != "" },
            text = { classroot ? "" + this.parent.parent.glyph : "" } ],

        // Drawn app art, authored in a 48-unit box and scaled to fill the plate.

        art: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height },
            visible = { classroot.kind != "" },

            // the illustration is drawn at the icon's LIVE size — a canvas
            // scale inside draw() (vector, recomputed every frame) rather than
            // a CSS transform stretching a fixed 48px raster, which re-samples
            // and jitters a pixel as the magnification glides. The body keeps
            // its 48-unit authoring coordinates; only the scale moved inward.

            draw(d) {
                d.scale(this.width / 48, this.width / 48)
                const k = classroot.kind
                if (k == "calendar") {
                    d.fillStyle = 0xFFFFFF
                    d.fillRect(0, 0, 48, 48)
                    d.fillStyle = 0xFB4238
                    d.fillRect(0, 0, 48, 13)
                    // the weekday and day are drawn in the SAME 48-unit space,
                    // so the whole face — band, MON, and the number — scales as
                    // one unit at any size (dock crest or badge); no per-glyph
                    // font clamp to skew their relative sizes.
                    d.textAlign = "center"
                    d.fillStyle = 0xFFFFFF
                    d.font = "bold 7.5px system-ui, sans-serif"
                    d.textBaseline = "middle"
                    d.fillText(app.dayName, 24, 7)
                    d.fillStyle = 0x1D1D1F
                    d.font = "27px system-ui, sans-serif"
                    d.fillText("" + app.dayNum, 24, 31)
                    }
                else { app.drawKindArt(d, k, classroot.hue1) }
                },
            ],

        // the calendar's live weekday + day are drawn inside `art` (one 48-unit
        // face that scales whole), not as separate Text children

        // The icon's glossy edge — the same top-lit rim the dock plate carries,
        // scaled to the icon and drawn at its live size (no raster jitter as it
        // magnifies). A 1px white highlight, brightest along the top and fading
        // down, like the sheen a glossy rounded tile catches from above.

        rim: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height },
            draw(d) {
                const w = this.width
                const h = this.height
                const r = w * 0.23
                const g = d.createLinearGradient(0, 0, 0, h)
                g.addColorStop(0, "rgba(255,255,255,0.42)")
                g.addColorStop(0.34, "rgba(255,255,255,0.07)")
                g.addColorStop(1, "rgba(255,255,255,0)")
                d.strokeStyle = g
                d.lineWidth = 1
                d.beginPath()
                d.roundRect(0.5, 0.5, w - 1, h - 1, r - 0.5)
                d.stroke()
                },
            ],
        ],

    // the running-app dot, centered in the strip below the icon

    // the dot marks is-running and never bounces: adding hopY back cancels the
    // icon's launch-bounce offset, pinning the dot to the strip

    dot: View [ width = 4, height = 4, cornerRadius = 2,
        x = { (parent.width - 4) / 2 }, y = { parent.height + 6 + this.parent.hopY },
        visible = { running }, fill = { theme.textMuted } ],

    // The label pill — the dock's own tooltip: a frosted chip with a downward
    // pointer tick and no shadow. It floats just above the hovered icon's own
    // top, so its tail tip meets the icon and — since the hovered icon is the
    // swell's peak — it clears every neighbor. As the icon magnifies and its top
    // rises, the pill rides up with it.

    pill: View [ visible = { hovered },
        width = { this.t.width + 26 }, height = 33,
        x = { (parent.width - this.width) / 2 },
        y = { parent.height - 130 },   // pinned to the shared pill baseline, so it lands right at any magnification
        bodyH: number = 22,
        draw(d) { app.drawPill(d, this.width, this.bodyH, app.darkMode) },
        t: Text [ x = center, y = { (this.parent.bodyH - this.height) / 2 }, fontSize = 13,
            textColor = { app.darkMode ? 0xF2F2F5 : 0x1D1D1F },
            text = { classroot ? "" + this.parent.parent.name : "" } ],
        ],
    ]

segments.md:
## App — the desktop root

The whole desktop: the wallpaper, the menu bar, the windows layer, and the
dock, plus the state and commands that tie them together. It tracks the browser
window with no size floor (a floor would make the page scroll, and a desktop
never scrolls), and the root clips, so a window hanging past an edge is cropped
where it lies rather than moved. Everything anchored to an edge — the menu bar's
width, the clock's right edge, the dock's seat at the bottom — re-derives from
app.width / app.height on every host resize.

segments.html:
App [ clip = true, appName = "Declare Desktop",
    theme = { Themes.cupertino(app.darkMode) },
    fontFamily = "system-ui, sans-serif",

    // a dark solid beneath the drawn wallpaper (the `wallpaper` view covers it;
    // this only shows before first paint / outside its bounds)

    fill = 0x0A0612,

    // Window-activation policy: a click on a background window only brings it
    // forward; the app inside hears nothing until the window is front. Flip to
    // false for click-through (the first click both activates and lands in the
    // content).

    firstClickActivatesOnly: boolean = true,

    // ── shared desktop geometry: named once, read by every constraint below ──

    menuBarH: number = 32,                       // the menu bar's height; windows live below it
    wallSeed: number = 7,                        // the generative wallpaper's seed; ` (backtick) cycles it
    iconEdge: number = 56,                       // a dock icon's resting edge
    dockFloor: number = 13,                      // the icons' resting bottom line, up from the desktop's bottom edge
    badgeEdge: number = 20,                      // a minimized window's app badge, at rest
    thumbEdge: number = 64,                      // a minimized thumbnail's longer edge — bigger than an icon, and centered

segments.md:
### The dock's magnification swell

The swell has two jobs, and each gets its own smoothing so the motion is
FRAME-paced, not pointer-EVENT-paced (browsers coalesce pointer events and
deliver them irregularly — a magnification read straight off `pointerX`
stutters, holding for several frames and then jumping):

  • dockX — a spring that chases the raw pointer. Everything the swell
    touches reads dockX, so it re-derives every frame the spring ticks (the
    shared clock delivers those smoothly), gliding between the sparse
    pointer events. Its slight trail is the settle you feel.
  • hot — the enter/exit envelope (on the dock view): 0 when the pointer is
    away from the dock band, 1 while over it. It scales the whole swell, so
    entering and leaving glide instead of snapping.

`dockMag` is the shape: distance-from-an-icon → magnification. The hovered
icon reaches about 1.55× and the swell falls off over four icon-pitches as a
pointed peak (a steep first step), set by the knots below. Distance is to
the RESTING center (cx0), never the magnified layout, so the swell can't
feed back on itself.

segments.html:
dockX: number = 0,

    // In-dock tracking: the swell sits under the cursor with about a frame of
    // lag and settles in ~110ms, critically damped (no tail).

    dockXSpring: Spring [ attribute = dockX, to = { app.pointerX }, stiffness = 1300, damping = 62, epsilon = 0.5 ],

    dockMag(dist) {
        const pitch = this.iconEdge + 16
        const a = Math.abs(dist)
        const xs = [0, pitch, 2 * pitch, 3 * pitch, 4 * pitch]
        // A pointed peak, not a dome: the hovered icon reaches 1.55× and the
        // drop-off is steepest on the first step.
        // peak · ±1 · ±2 · ±3 · ±4 = 1.55 · 1.35 · 1.18 · 1.06 · 1.0.
        const ys = [1.55, 1.35, 1.18, 1.06, 1]
        if (a >= xs[4]) return 1
        for (let i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
            if (a <= xs[i + 1]) return ys[i] + (ys[i + 1] - ys[i]) * (a - xs[i]) / (xs[i + 1] - xs[i])
            }
        return 1
        },

    // an app illustration in the 48-unit authoring box (the caller has already
    // scaled to size, and painted the plate hue behind). The plate-hued `kind`s
    // only — the live calendar face is drawn by each caller, which owns the day
    // data. ONE renderer, shared by the dock icons and the "Not implemented"
    // alert, so an app's icon is identical wherever it appears.

    drawKindArt(d, kind, hue1) {
        const rr = (x, y, w, h, r) => {
            d.moveTo(x + r, y)
            d.lineTo(x + w - r, y); d.arc(x + w - r, y + r, r, -Math.PI / 2, 0, false)
            d.lineTo(x + w, y + h - r); d.arc(x + w - r, y + h - r, r, 0, Math.PI / 2, false)
            d.lineTo(x + r, y + h); d.arc(x + r, y + h - r, r, Math.PI / 2, Math.PI, false)
            d.lineTo(x, y + r); d.arc(x + r, y + r, r, Math.PI, Math.PI * 1.5, false)
            d.closePath()
            }
        if (kind == "files") {
            d.fillStyle = 0xD9E7FA
            d.beginPath()
            rr(9, 13.5, 14, 8, 2)
            d.fill()
            d.beginPath()
            rr(9, 16, 30, 19, 2.5)
            d.fill()
            d.fillStyle = 0xFFFFFF
            d.beginPath()
            rr(9, 20, 30, 15, 2.5)
            d.fill()
            }
        else if (kind == "mail") {
            d.fillStyle = 0xFFFFFF
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo(9, 15); d.lineTo(39, 15); d.lineTo(39, 34); d.lineTo(9, 34)
            d.closePath(); d.fill()
            d.strokeStyle = 0xB9C6DB
            d.lineWidth = 1.6
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo(9.5, 15.8); d.lineTo(24, 26); d.lineTo(38.5, 15.8)
            d.stroke()
            }
        else if (kind == "terminal") {
            d.strokeStyle = 0xFFFFFF
            d.lineWidth = 2.6
            d.lineCap = "round"
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo(14, 18.5); d.lineTo(20, 24); d.lineTo(14, 29.5)
            d.moveTo(24, 30); d.lineTo(32.5, 30)
            d.stroke()
            }
        else if (kind == "pencil") {
            // the pencil: eraser upper-left, tip lower-right. Body + tip in
            // white; the eraser seam struck back in the plate's hue.
            d.fillStyle = 0xFFFFFF
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo(7.8, 12.2); d.lineTo(14.2, 5.8); d.lineTo(35.7, 27.3); d.lineTo(29.3, 33.7)
            d.closePath(); d.fill()
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo(35.7, 27.3); d.lineTo(29.3, 33.7); d.lineTo(39.5, 37.5)
            d.closePath(); d.fill()
            d.strokeStyle = hue1 != null ? hue1 : 0xFFFFFF   // hue is always supplied; the guard just narrows the nullable Color param
            d.lineWidth = 1.6
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo(11.4, 15.7); d.lineTo(17.7, 9.4)
            d.moveTo(13.5, 17.8); d.lineTo(19.8, 11.5)
            d.stroke()
            }
        else if (kind == "jots") {
            // all-yellow paper (the plate's yellow is the background), a
            // saturated strip and faint ruled lines
            d.fillStyle = 0xF3DC49
            d.fillRect(0, 0, 48, 10)
            d.strokeStyle = 0xE0CE66
            d.lineWidth = 2
            d.beginPath()
            d.moveTo(8, 19); d.lineTo(40, 19)
            d.moveTo(8, 27); d.lineTo(40, 27)
            d.moveTo(8, 35); d.lineTo(26, 35)
            d.stroke()
            }
        },

    // The label pill's silhouette — ONE path: a stadium body (fully-rounded
    // left/right ends, r = half the body height, so the caps are true
    // semicircles, not rounded-rectangle corners) plus a small fixed triangle
    // tail at bottom-center. Filled translucent and stroked as a single outline,
    // so the border wraps the tail with no seam. The body is ~22px, the tail
    // ~7px on a ~16px base, and the fill is ~0.88 so the wallpaper shows through.
    // The tail is fixed — it doesn't grow with the name.

    drawPill(d, w, bh, dark) {
        const r = bh / 2                     // stadium: fully-rounded ends (semicircle caps)
        const th = 7                         // FIXED triangle height — does NOT scale with the name
        const hb = 8                         // FIXED triangle half-base (narrow)
        const cx = w / 2
        d.beginPath()
        d.moveTo(r, 0)
        d.lineTo(w - r, 0)                                       // top edge
        d.arc(w - r, r, r, -Math.PI / 2, Math.PI / 2, false)    // right cap (semicircle)
        d.lineTo(cx + hb, bh)                                    // bottom edge → triangle base
        d.lineTo(cx, bh + th)                                    // down to the tip
        d.lineTo(cx - hb, bh)                                    // back up
        d.lineTo(r, bh)                                          // bottom edge
        d.arc(r, r, r, Math.PI / 2, Math.PI * 1.5, false)       // left cap (semicircle)
        d.closePath()
        d.fillStyle = dark ? "rgba(46,46,51,0.9)" : "rgba(242,242,246,0.88)"
        d.fill()
        d.strokeStyle = dark ? "rgba(0,0,0,0.35)" : "rgba(196,196,204,0.85)"
        d.lineWidth = 1
        d.stroke()
        },

    // Activation is one View-typed slot: every window derives its look (shadow
    // depth, bar tone, controls) from whether it is this value.

    frontWin: View = null,
    winCount: number = 0,                        // ever-opened count — staggers spawn positions
    viewerCount: number = 0,                     // open viewers, for the dock's running dot

segments.md:
### The application model

Several "applications" share this desktop — Files, Markdown (the viewer),
Declare Calendar and Declare Viewer (hosted apps), and Jots — and the
menu bar is contextual: it belongs to the front window's application. A
window's app is read from its class; About windows carry theirs explicitly.

segments.html:
pathOf(w) { return w != null && w.appPath ? "" + w.appPath : "" },   // any-seam: an AppWindow's program

    appOf(w) {
        if (w == null) return "files"
        const c = w.constructor.name
        if (c == "ViewerWindow") return "markdown"
        if (c == "JotWindow") return "jots"
        if (c == "AppWindow") return this.pathOf(w).indexOf("viewer") >= 0 ? "declareviewer" : "calendar"
        if (c == "AboutWindow") return "" + w.forApp
        return "files"
        },

    activeApp: string = { this.appOf(app.frontWin) },

    nameOf(a) {
        return a == "markdown" ? "Markdown" : a == "calendar" ? "Declare Calendar"
            : a == "declareviewer" ? "Declare Viewer"
            : a == "jots" ? "Jots"
            : a == "desktop" ? "Declare Desktop" : "Files"
        },

    // View & Edit Source opens the running program itself in the Declare
    // Viewer — as an embedded app in a desktop window: the AppWindow hosts
    // apps/viewer, and `program=` in its env segment names the subject (a
    // path valid in this app's transport base). The viewer self-loads that
    // program's ?segments and ?file artifacts — reader mode by default.

    sourceRel(a) {
        return a == "calendar" ? "../calendar/calendar.declare"
            : a == "declareviewer" ? "../viewer/viewer.declare"
            : "desktop.declare"
        },

    openSource(a) {
        this.openApp("../../viewer/viewer", "Declare Viewer", null, "program=" + this.sourceRel(a))
        },

    // ── the command seam: the menu bar, the dock, and keys all route here ──

    command(id) {
        if (id == "new") {
            if (activeApp == "markdown") this.openViewer("../../docs/declare.md", "declare.md")
            else if (activeApp == "calendar") this.openApp("../../calendar/calendar", "Calendar", null, "base=../calendar/")
            else if (activeApp == "declareviewer") this.openApp("../../viewer/viewer", "Declare Viewer", null, "program=desktop.declare")
            else if (activeApp == "jots") this.newJot("", null)
            else this.newFiles()
            }
        else if (id == "open") {
            if (activeApp == "declareviewer") this.openApp("../../viewer/viewer", "Declare Viewer", null, "program=desktop.declare")
            else if (activeApp == "jots") this.newJot("", null)
            else this.openViewer("../../docs/declare.md", "declare.md")
            }
        else if (id == "close") { if (frontWin != null) this.closeWin(frontWin) }
        else if (id == "dark") { app.darkChoice = !app.darkMode; app.darkLocked = true }
        else if (id == "about-desktop") this.openAbout("desktop")
        else if (id == "about-app") this.openAbout(this.activeApp)
        else if (id == "source-desktop") this.openSource("desktop")
