        else if (id == "home") app.navigate("apps/homepage/homepage.declare")   // distro-root-relative → works on dev server AND static host
        else if (id == "source") this.openSource(this.activeApp)
        else if (id == "quit") this.quitApp(this.activeApp)
        else if (id.indexOf("win:") == 0) this.focusWin(this.wins.children[+id.slice(4)])
        },

    focusWin(w) { if (w == null) return; if (this.isMin(w)) this.restoreMini(w); else w.activate() },

    // Quit closes every window of that application — each by its own reverse
    // zoom — and clears its running flag (Files has no Quit; it's always
    // running). Running is an application state, not a window count: closing the
    // last window leaves the app running, its dock dot lit — only Quit ends it.

    quitApp(a) {
        const doomed = this.wins.children.filter((w) => this.appOf(w) == a)
        for (const w of doomed) this.closeWin(w)
        if (a == "markdown") mdRunning = false
        else if (a == "calendar") calRunning = false
        else if (a == "declareviewer") dvRunning = false
        else if (a == "jots") jotRunning = false
        },

    // A dock icon's click: a running app's icon raises its open windows (in
    // their stacking order, so the front one ends front); with none open —
    // running or not — the app's default open runs instead.

    dockOpen(a, open) {
        const live = this.wins.children.filter((w) => this.appOf(w) == a && !this.isPlain(w) && !this.isMin(w))
        if (live.length > 0) { for (const w of live) this.focusWin(w); return }
        // nothing up but something in the dock: restore the most recent tile
        const mins = this.miniList().filter((w) => this.appOf(w) == a)
        if (mins.length > 0) { this.restoreMini(mins[mins.length - 1]); return }
        open()
        },

segments.md:
### Minimize to dock

A minimized window becomes a dock citizen: its geometry constraints run the
icons' own swell math, so it magnifies and displaces along with the row, and
the dock's span is sprung so the plate widens smoothly to admit it. The
records here drive the invisible hit tiles and their label pills.

segments.html:
isMin(w) { return w != null && w.dockSlot != null && w.dockSlot >= 0 },
    ixOf(w) { return w != null && w.dockSlot != null ? +w.dockSlot : -1 },
    setIx(w, i) { w.dockSlot = i },
    parkOf(w) { return w != null && w.miniT != null ? +w.miniT : 0 },
    labelFor(w) { const t = this.titleOf(w); return t != "" ? t : this.nameOf(this.appOf(w)) },
    miniList() { return this.wins.children.filter((w) => this.isMin(w)).sort((a, b) => this.ixOf(a) - this.ixOf(b)) },

    miniCount: number = 0,
    miniRecs: array = null,

    // compact the slot indices and rebuild the tile records — after every
    // park, unpark, and close-of-parked. Slot widths are per window: the
    // thumbnail keeps its window's aspect, its longer edge matching an app
    // icon's edge, so it is never wider or taller than an icon.

    sizeOf(w) { return ({ w: w.width, h: w.height }) },

    // miniWins mirrors the records by index with the window references — a
    // parallel app attr, kept out of the records: records are data, and a
    // View's circular graph must never enter data-shaped state

    miniWins: array = null,

    rebuildMinis() {
        const ws = this.miniList()
        const out = []
        let total = 0
        for (let i = 0; i < ws.length; i++) {
            this.setIx(ws[i], i)
            const z = this.sizeOf(ws[i])
            // the longer edge matches an app icon's edge: a minimized
            // window is never wider or taller than an icon
            const fit = this.thumbEdge / Math.max(z.w, z.h)
            const slotW = Math.round(z.w * fit)
            const slotH = Math.round(z.h * fit)
            out.push(({ ix: i, label: this.labelFor(ws[i]), kind: this.appOf(ws[i]), w: slotW, h: slotH }))
            total += slotW + 8
            }
        miniRecs = out
        miniWins = ws
        miniCount = out.length
        // the mini section's resting width added to the plate: the divider zone
        // (34 = 16px left margin + line + 18px right) plus the slots and their
        // gaps. `total` already counts a trailing 8px gap, so: total + 34 − 8.
        miniGoal = out.length > 0 ? total + 26 : 0
        },

    // the minimized section's width — sprung toward the rebuilt goal, so
    // admitting or releasing a tile widens and narrows the dock as motion

    miniGoal: number = 0,
    miniSpan: number = 0,
    spanSpring: Spring [ attribute = miniSpan, to = { app.miniGoal }, stiffness = 380, damping = 34, epsilon = 0.5 ],

    // ── the mini layout: one computation both the parked windows and their
    // hit tiles read (deps arrive as arguments, so the constraint visibly
    // tracks them — the windowItems pattern). Left-anchored accumulation,
    // exactly like the icon row's layout: a magnified thumbnail pushes its
    // neighbors right and never crosses the divider on its left. The wave's
    // falloff runs over resting centers (the icons' anti-feedback rule).

    miniLayout: array = { this.computeMiniLayout(app.miniRecs, app.dockX, app.dock.hot,
        app.dock.x, app.dock.row.width, app.miniSpan, app.width) },

    // iconRow = the nine icons + eight gaps at rest; 10 = the plate's side
    // skirts (5 each); 5 = the row's inset in the plate; 17 = the divider
    // zone (8 + line + 8). The wave itself is app.dockMag — one curve, read
    // here for the tiles and by each DockIcon for the icons.

    computeMiniLayout(recs, px, hot, dockLeftX, rowW, span, appW) {
        const rs = recs || []
        const iconRow = 9 * app.iconEdge + 8 * 16   // the nine icons + eight gaps, at rest
        // 32 = the plate's two 16px side skirts; 16 = the row's inset in the
        // plate; 34 = the divider zone (16px left margin + line + 18px right
        // margin) — the first thumbnail starts 34px past the last icon's edge
        const restDockX = (appW - (iconRow + 32 + span)) / 2
        let rx = restDockX + 16 + iconRow + 34      // first slot's resting left
        let lx = dockLeftX + 16 + rowW + 34         // …and its live left
        const out = []
        for (const r of rs) {
            const cx0 = rx + r.w / 2
            // the same wave the icons ride (app.dockMag), so tiles magnify in step
            const m = 1 + (app.dockMag(px - cx0) - 1) * hot
            const w = r.w * m
            out.push(({ x: lx, w: w, rw: r.w, h: (r.h || r.w) * m, cx: lx + w / 2 }))
            lx += w + 8
            rx += r.w + 8
            }
        return out
        },

    // the wave's extra width beyond rest — the plate stretches for it, so a
    // magnified thumbnail never hangs past the dock's right edge

    miniMagX: number = { this.magOf(app.miniLayout, app.miniRecs) },
    magOf(lay, recs) {
        if (lay == null || recs == null || lay.length == 0) return 0
        let d = 0
        for (let i = 0; i < lay.length; i++) d += lay[i].w - recs[i].w
        return Math.max(0, d)
        },

    startPark(w) { w.miniBack.stop(); w.bornX = 0; w.bornY = 0; w.miniGo.start() },
    startUnpark(w) { w.miniGo.stop(); w.miniBack.start() },

    // settled-park state rides the records (the badge derives from its
    // record's `full`, plain data — no reactive aggregation in constraints):
    // full only after the arriving journey completes, and cleared the
    // instant the journey home begins

    homingOf(w) { return w != null && w.homing == true },
    setHoming(w, v) { w.homing = v },

    minimizeWin(w) {
        if (w == null || this.isPlain(w) || this.isMin(w)) return
        if (this.miniCount >= 6) return          // the static tile pool's size
        this.setIx(w, this.miniCount)
        this.rebuildMinis()
        if (frontWin == w) frontWin = null
        w.raise()                                // the thumbnail pins to the top of the minimized band
        this.startPark(w)
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        },

    bringFront(w) { w.activate() },              // any-seam (focusWin would recurse via isMin)

    // ── The minimized band: minimized windows' live thumbnails stay in `wins`
    // (below the plate) but pin to the TOP of the layer, so a full-size window
    // that reaches the dock can't cover them. A window that activates or opens
    // raises only to just BENEATH the band — `raise(below)` with the band's
    // floor — so the band always wins. `lowestMini` is that floor: the lowest-z
    // SETTLED minimized window (a homing or restoring window is leaving the
    // band, so it's excluded, and the restoring window comes up to the front of
    // the non-minimized band).

    lowestMini() {
        const ws = this.wins.children
        for (let i = 0; i < ws.length; i++) { const w = ws[i]; if (this.isMin(w) && !this.homingOf(w)) return w }
        return null
        },

    // bring a freshly-opened window to the front of the NON-minimized band

    frontOpen(w) { frontWin = w; w.raise(this.lowestMini()) },

    // seatedness for a tile's badge: the window's visual width equals the
    // slot's — geometry, not bookkeeping. Deps arrive as arguments (the
    // windowItems pattern); winSeq re-derives it at every journey boundary
    // (miniGo's completion nudges it), pointer/hot cover the wave frames.

    seatCheck(wins, i, slotW, seq, px, hot) {
        const w = wins != null && i >= 0 && i < wins.length ? wins[i] : null
        if (w == null || w.homing == true) return false   // homing = leaving now: the badge goes first
        return w.scale < 0.9 && Math.abs(w.scale * w.width - slotW) < 0.5
        },

    nudge() { winSeq = winSeq + 1 },

    restoreMini(w) {
        if (w == null || !this.isMin(w) || this.homingOf(w)) return   // already on the way home
        this.setHoming(w, true)
        this.startUnpark(w)                      // the slot stays mine until the journey home ends
        this.bringFront(w)                       // frontmost from the first frame of the journey
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        },

    // miniBack's onStop: the window is home — release the slot and focus it

    unparked(w) {
        this.setIx(w, -1)
        this.setHoming(w, false)
        this.rebuildMinis()
        this.focusWin(w)
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        },

    restoreMiniAt(i) {
        const ws = this.miniList()
        if (i >= 0 && i < ws.length) this.restoreMini(ws[i])
        },

    openAbout(a) {
        winCount = winCount + 1
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        const w = this.createView("AboutWindow", this.wins, ({ forApp: "" + a,
            wx: (app.width - 280) / 2 + (winCount % 3) * 16, wy: 110 + (winCount % 3) * 14 }))
        this.frontOpen(w)
        },

    // a press that lands on nothing deeper (the wallpaper) also dismisses —
    // the App root is the sink of last resort

    dismissAbouts() {
        const doomed = this.wins.children.filter((w) => this.isPlain(w))
        for (const w of doomed) this.closeWin(w)
        },

    onMouseDown() { this.dismissAbouts() },

    // Appearance follows the OS color scheme (app.dark) by default; the menu's
    // Dark / Light item locks the choice to the user's pick from that point on,
    // so later system flips no longer move it.

    darkLocked: boolean = false,
    darkChoice: boolean = false,
    darkMode: boolean = { this.darkLocked ? this.darkChoice : app.dark },

    // ── The "Not implemented" alert: one app-modal sheet the inert dock apps
    // raise. A single slot of state — `notImpl` fills it (name + icon), and the
    // OK button clears it. `alertName == ""` means no alert.

    alertName: string = "",
    alertHead: string = "",                      // the terse per-app headline ("No." / "Nope." / …)
    alertKind: string = "",
    alertGlyph: string = "",
    alertGsize: number = 0.42,
    alertHue1: Color = #4A90D9,
    alertHue2: Color = #2E5FA3,
    notImpl(head, name, kind, glyph, gsize, h1, h2) {
        alertHead = head
        alertName = name
        alertKind = kind
        alertGlyph = glyph
        alertGsize = gsize
        alertHue1 = h1
        alertHue2 = h2
        },
    dismissAlert() { alertName = "" },

    newFiles() {
        this.dismissAbouts()
        winCount = winCount + 1
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        // spawn positions cascade, wrapping after a handful
        const w = this.createView("FilesWindow", this.wins, ({ wx: 90 + (winCount % 7) * 28, wy: 46 + (winCount % 7) * 26 }))
        this.frontOpen(w)
        },

    // Every open verb takes `v`, the view the open came from (the double-clicked
    // row or icon): the window zooms out of it and shrinks back to it on close.
    // With no origin (a menu, the dock, ⌘O) a window zooms gently from its own
    // center. originOf resolves that center in app coords by walking the parents.

    originOf(v) {
        if (v == null) return null
        let ox = v.width / 2
        let oy = v.height / 2
        let n = v
        while (n != null) { ox += n.x || 0; oy += n.y || 0; n = n.parent }
        return ({ x: ox, y: oy })
        },

    // A first open (count 0 — or 0 again after a Quit) is a launch: the dock
    // icon bounces and the window appears mid-second-bounce, the way a
    // fast-launching app feels. Later opens are instant.

    openViewer(p, nm, v) {
        if (p == "") return
        const first = !mdRunning && !mdLaunching
        if (first) mdLaunching = true
        const go = () => {
            mdLaunching = false
            mdRunning = true
            viewerCount = viewerCount + 1
            this.dismissAbouts()
            winCount = winCount + 1
            winSeq = winSeq + 1
            const wx = 160 + (winCount % 7) * 28
            const wy = 36 + (winCount % 7) * 26
            const o = this.originOf(v)
            const w = this.createView("ViewerWindow", this.wins, ({ path: p, wx: wx, wy: wy,
                bornX: o != null ? o.x - wx : -1, bornY: o != null ? o.y - wy - this.menuBarH : -1,
                bornFrom: v != null ? v : null }))
            this.frontOpen(w)
            }
        if (first) { this.dock.row.viewerIcon.poke(); setTimeout(go, 900) }
        else go()
        },

    // open a HYDRATED document — Markdown the model already assembled (an
    // element's reference, the enums page). The same window as openViewer, but
    // the page is handed over whole instead of a path to fetch.

    openDoc(md, nm, v) {
        if (md == "") return
        const first = !mdRunning && !mdLaunching
        if (first) mdLaunching = true
        const go = () => {
            mdLaunching = false
            mdRunning = true
            viewerCount = viewerCount + 1
            this.dismissAbouts()
            winCount = winCount + 1
            winSeq = winSeq + 1
            const wx = 160 + (winCount % 7) * 28
            const wy = 36 + (winCount % 7) * 26
            const o = this.originOf(v)
            const w = this.createView("ViewerWindow", this.wins, ({ inlineDoc: "" + md, docName: "" + nm, wx: wx, wy: wy,
                bornX: o != null ? o.x - wx : -1, bornY: o != null ? o.y - wy - this.menuBarH : -1,
                bornFrom: v != null ? v : null }))
            this.frontOpen(w)
            }
        if (first) { this.dock.row.viewerIcon.poke(); setTimeout(go, 900) }
        else go()
        },

    // a full Declare app in a window — the path is demos/-relative (the slot
    // convention): "../../calendar/calendar" runs the real calendar,
    // "../../viewer/viewer" the Declare Viewer. `envExtra` appends to the
    // window's env segment ("program=…"). The two hosted apps count
    // separately (running dots + Quit are per application), and each has a
    // dock icon that launch-bounces on its first open.

    calCount: number = 0,
    dvCount: number = 0,

    // running is per application and survives its windows: set when a launch
    // completes, cleared only by Quit — the dock dots read exactly these

    mdRunning: boolean = false,
    calRunning: boolean = false,
    dvRunning: boolean = false,
    jotRunning: boolean = false,

    launching: boolean = false,                  // calendar mid-launch-bounce
    dvLaunching: boolean = false,                // Declare Viewer mid-launch-bounce
    mdLaunching: boolean = false,                // markdown mid-launch-bounce
    jotLaunching: boolean = false,                // Jots mid-launch-bounce

    openApp(p, nm, v, envExtra) {
        const isCal = ("" + p).indexOf("calendar") >= 0
        // the launch gate: flags move inside go(), so the running dot appears
        // when the app has launched (mid-second-bounce), not at the click —
        // the mid-launch flag guards a double-click during the bounce
        const first = !(isCal ? calRunning : dvRunning) && !(isCal ? launching : dvLaunching)
        if (first) { if (isCal) launching = true; else dvLaunching = true }
        const go = () => {
            if (isCal) { launching = false; calRunning = true; calCount = calCount + 1 }
            else { dvLaunching = false; dvRunning = true; dvCount = dvCount + 1 }
            this.dismissAbouts()
            winCount = winCount + 1
            winSeq = winSeq + 1
            let wx = 110 + (winCount % 5) * 30
            let wy = 24 + (winCount % 5) * 22
            let ww = 880, wh = 560
            // The calendar opens smaller than a full app window and clears the
            // dock (its height caps to the room above it), centered
            // horizontally. The viewer keeps its base size.
            if (isCal) {
                wy = 28
                // clear the dock (height caps to the room above it) AND never
                // fall below the calendar's own min (480 × 420) — clamp to both.
                ww = Math.max(480, Math.min(920, this.width - 80))
                wh = Math.max(420, Math.min(600, this.height - this.menuBarH - wy - 168))   // 168 ≈ dock footprint + gap
                wx = Math.round((this.width - ww) / 2)
                }
            const o = this.originOf(v)
            const w = this.createView("AppWindow", this.wins, ({ appPath: "" + p, appTitle: "" + nm, wx: wx, wy: wy,
                width: ww, height: wh,
                extraEnv: envExtra != null ? "" + envExtra : "",
                bornX: o != null ? o.x - wx : -1, bornY: o != null ? o.y - wy - this.menuBarH : -1,
                bornFrom: v != null ? v : null }))
            this.frontOpen(w)
            }
        if (first) { (isCal ? this.dock.row.calIcon : this.dock.row.dvIcon).poke(); setTimeout(go, 900) }
        else go()
        },

    // ── Jots: the built-in application — its notes are JotWindows in
    // this very program, no island involved. The welcome note pre-opens at boot
    // (so Jots starts running, its dot lit, with no bounce — it was already
    // up when you arrived); later launches bounce like any app's.

    welcomeNote: string = """
**Hello!** This is a working desktop — windows, menus, a dock — running entirely in your browser.

It's built with **Declare**, a new language for user interfaces. A Declare program doesn't script behavior; it states what the interface *is* — this note's yellow, that window's shadow — and the runtime keeps every relationship true while you interact.

**Try things.** Drag the windows. Open apps from the dock. Minimize something. Browse the language's documentation in the Documentation window.

**Curious how it's made?** Press the `[ ]` icon in the dock to read this desktop's own source.
""",

    newJot(txt, v) {
        const first = !jotRunning && !jotLaunching
        if (first) jotLaunching = true
        const go = () => {
            jotLaunching = false
            jotRunning = true
            this.dismissAbouts()
            winCount = winCount + 1
            winSeq = winSeq + 1
            const w = this.createView("JotWindow", this.wins, ({ note: "" + (txt || "*A new note.*"),
                wx: 480 + (winCount % 5) * 26, wy: 90 + (winCount % 5) * 24 }))
            this.frontOpen(w)
            }
        if (first) { this.dock.row.jotIcon.poke(); setTimeout(go, 900) }
        else go()
        },

    openWelcome() {
        jotRunning = true
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        const w = this.createView("JotWindow", this.wins, ({ note: this.welcomeNote,
            width: 400, height: 345, wx: Math.max(440, app.width - 490), wy: 120 }))
        this.frontOpen(w)
        },

    closeWin(w) { w.beginClose() },              // the reverse zoom; removal on its onStop

    reallyClose(w) {
        winSeq = winSeq + 1
        if (w.constructor.name == "AppWindow") {
            if (this.appOf(w) == "calendar") calCount = calCount - 1
            else dvCount = dvCount - 1
            }
        if (frontWin == w) frontWin = null
        if (w.constructor.name == "ViewerWindow") viewerCount = viewerCount - 1
        this.wins.removeChild(w)
        w.discard()
        this.rebuildMinis()                      // a Quit can take a minimized window's tile with it
        },

    // The menu-bar clock is live — a static time would be the first thing to
    // give the desktop away.

    clockText: string = "",

    dayName: string = "",
    dayNum: number = 0,

    tick() {
        const d = new Date()
        const wd = ["Sun", "Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat"][d.getDay()]
        const mo = ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"][d.getMonth()]
        const h12 = d.getHours() % 12 == 0 ? 12 : d.getHours() % 12
        const mm = (d.getMinutes() < 10 ? "0" : "") + d.getMinutes()
        clockText = wd + " " + mo + " " + d.getDate() + "  " + h12 + ":" + mm + " " + (d.getHours() >= 12 ? "PM" : "AM")
        dayName = wd.toUpperCase()
        dayNum = d.getDate()
        },

    // ── The documentation model: one fetch of the docs app's own spine. The
    // Files browser's Guide and Reference folders, and the synthesized reference
    // pages, all derive from it.

    docsModel: DataSource [ url = "../../docs/declare-model.json" ],

    // 15s ticks catch the minute flip well within a minute-precision display

    // Both boot windows open one tick later: the welcome note's position
    // derives from app.width, which is not yet live inside onInit (the host
    // wires sizes after construction) — an immediate open would land on the
    // fallback floor. Order is deliberate: the note opens first, then the Files
    // window on top of it, so you arrive with Files frontmost and the greeting
    // resting behind.

    onInit() { this.docsModel.fetch(); setTimeout(() => { this.openWelcome(); this.newFiles() }, 0); this.tick(); setInterval(() => this.tick(), 15000) },

    // ── ⌘-shortcuts dispatch from the menus' own records: one registry, derived
    // from whatever the contextual bar currently shows, so the active
    // application's keys are automatically the live set — nothing is declared
    // twice.

    keyRegistry: array = { (app.menus || [])
        .flatMap((m) => (m.items || []).filter((r) => r.key).map((r) => ({ menu: "" + m.label, ...r }))) },

    onKeyDown(e) <- Keys {
        if (e.key == "`" && !e.meta && !e.repeat) { app.wallSeed = app.wallSeed + 1; return }   // cycle the wallpaper (selection/debug)
        if (!e.meta || e.repeat || e.key == "Meta") return
        if (this.bar.mb.shown) return                // an open menu owns the keyboard
        const combo = "⌘" + (e.key.length == 1 ? e.key.toUpperCase() : e.key)
        const hit = (this.keyRegistry || []).find((r) => r.key == combo && r.enabled != false)
        if (hit != null) this.command("" + hit.id)
        },

segments.md:
### The wallpaper — a generative mesh field, drawn

A hue-tinted dark ground with five saturated color poles, biased to the edges
and melted together with a big blur() under a `lighten` blend — a bold,
cohesive gradient field. It is a PURE FUNCTION of `app.wallSeed`: a seeded
PRNG picks the palette (a per-seed color scheme keeps hues cohesive rather
than muddy) and the pole layout, so pressing ` (backtick) bumps the seed and
rotates through the generated space. It has no handler, so a desktop press
still dismisses an About box. Only draw()-subset primitives are used: radial
gradients, filter blur(), and globalCompositeOperation — no images.

**Drawn once, then paint-scaled.** The mesh is recorded in a FIXED reference
box (refW × refH constants), so its display list never changes with the
window — only `app.wallSeed` rebuilds it. On resize the view is cover-scaled
to fill (uniform, aspect preserved: the tighter axis fits, the other overflows
and clips) and re-centered, all with paint-only transforms, so the field never
re-rasters on resize. This matters because WebKit's 2D canvas is deferred —
draw() only records, and the raster flushes lazily — so re-recording this
field every resize frame (five full-screen radial fills plus a blur) runs
~24ms per flush in Safari (~42fps), stalling the whole compositor so the dock
trails the window; a cached raster plus a CSS scale is ~17ms (~60fps).
(Chrome's GPU raster is eager — ~8ms either way — which is why the lag never
showed there.) It's the same trick the dock icons use: draw once, then scale
with a paint-only transform. 1920×1200 covers a common maximized window at
scale ≤ 1 (crisp) and upscales gently past it (a blurred field hides the
softness).

segments.html:
wallpaper: View [ refW: number = 1920,
        refH: number = 1200,
        width = { this.refW }, height = { this.refH },
        x = { (app.width - this.refW) / 2 }, y = { (app.height - this.refH) / 2 },
        pivotX = { this.refW / 2 }, pivotY = { this.refH / 2 },
        scale = { Math.max(app.width / this.refW, app.height / this.refH) },
        draw(d) {
            const W = this.width
            const H = this.height
            const hsl = (h, s, l) => "hsl(" + (((h % 360) + 360) % 360) + "," + s + "%," + l + "%)"
            // seeded PRNG (mulberry32) — deterministic per app.wallSeed
            let a = (app.wallSeed >>> 0) || 1
            const rnd = () => {
                a = (a + 1831565813) | 0                          // mulberry32's increment, in decimal (an 8-digit 0x here would read as a color)
                let t = Math.imul(a ^ (a >>> 15), 1 | a)
                t = (t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), 61 | t)) ^ t
                return ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296
                }
            const hue0 = rnd() * 360
            // a COHESIVE color scheme per seed, so complementaries don't meet
            // and mud out; hue0 and the scheme swing strongly seed to seed
            const scheme = Math.floor(rnd() * 3)                 // 0 analogous · 1 triad · 2 split-complementary
            const hueFor = () => {
                if (scheme == 0) return hue0 + (rnd() - 0.5) * 100
                if (scheme == 1) return hue0 + [0, 120, 240][Math.floor(rnd() * 3)] + (rnd() - 0.5) * 26
                return rnd() < 0.72 ? hue0 + (rnd() - 0.5) * 74 : hue0 + 180 + (rnd() - 0.5) * 40
                }
            // dark, hue-tinted ground
            d.fillStyle = hsl(hue0, 52, 5)
            d.fillRect(0, 0, W, H)
            // five poles biased to the edges and corners, so the center never piles to white
            const spots = [[0.17, 0.24], [0.82, 0.2], [0.5, 0.54], [0.2, 0.82], [0.83, 0.8]]
            const blurPx = Math.round(Math.max(W, H) * 0.05)
            d.save()
            d.filter = "blur(" + blurPx + "px)"
            d.globalCompositeOperation = "lighten"                // per-channel max keeps overlaps saturated, not gray
            for (const s of spots) {
                const px = (s[0] + (rnd() - 0.5) * 0.18) * W
                const py = (s[1] + (rnd() - 0.5) * 0.18) * H
                const r = (0.36 + rnd() * 0.26) * Math.max(W, H)
                const h = hueFor()
                const sat = 70 + rnd() * 22
                const li = 44 + rnd() * 12
                const g = d.createRadialGradient(px, py, 0, px, py, r)
                g.addColorStop(0, hsl(h, sat, li))
                g.addColorStop(0.5, hsl(h, sat, li * 0.62))
                g.addColorStop(1, "rgba(0,0,0,0)")
                d.fillStyle = g
                d.fillRect(0, 0, W, H)
                }
            d.restore()
            // strong vignette — contrast, framing, and a darker center-out that
            // keeps the field from washing out
            const v = d.createRadialGradient(W / 2, H * 0.45, H * 0.1, W / 2, H * 0.5, W * 0.72)
            v.addColorStop(0, "rgba(0,0,0,0)")
            v.addColorStop(1, "rgba(0,0,6,0.62)")
            d.fillStyle = v
            d.fillRect(0, 0, W, H)
            // a faint top scrim so the (background-less) menu bar's light text
            // stays legible over any palette
            const top = d.createLinearGradient(0, 0, 0, 48)
            top.addColorStop(0, "rgba(0,0,0,0.30)")
            top.addColorStop(1, "rgba(0,0,0,0)")
            d.fillStyle = top
            d.fillRect(0, 0, W, 48)
            },
        ],

    // ── the windows layer: a structural container; order is promotion ──────

    wins: View [ x = 0, y = { app.menuBarH }, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height - app.menuBarH } ],

    winSeq: number = 0,                          // bumped on every open/close, so window lists re-derive

segments.md:
### The menu bar — whole menus as records, contextual

The `menus` array re-derives from the active application (and from winSeq /
frontWin for the Window menu), so the bar follows focus live, even while a
menu is open. Keyboard shortcuts dispatch from these same records.

segments.html:
menus: array = { [
        ({ id: "brand", label: "Declare", kind: "BrandMark", items: [
            ({ id: "about-desktop", label: "About Declare Desktop", icon: "[ ]" }),
            ({ divider: true }),
            ({ id: "dark", label: app.darkMode ? "Light Mode" : "Dark Mode", key: "⌘K", icon: "◐" }),
            ({ divider: true }),
            ({ id: "source-desktop", label: "View & Edit Source", icon: "‹›" }),
            ({ id: "home", label: "Declare Home" }),
        ] }),
        ({ id: "app", label: this.nameOf(this.activeApp), bold: true,
            items: this.appMenuItems(this.activeApp) }),
        ({ id: "file", label: "File", items: [
            ({ id: "new", label: "New", key: "⌘N" }),
            ({ id: "open", label: "Open…", key: "⌘O", enabled: this.activeApp != "calendar" }),
            ({ divider: true }),
            ({ id: "close", label: "Close Window", key: "⌘W", enabled: app.frontWin != null }),
        ] }),

        // the standard Edit suite — present and keyed, honestly disabled:
        // nothing on this desktop edits yet

        ({ id: "edit", label: "Edit", items: [
            ({ id: "undo", label: "Undo", key: "⌘Z", enabled: false }),
            ({ id: "redo", label: "Redo", key: "⇧⌘Z", enabled: false }),
            ({ divider: true }),
            ({ id: "cut", label: "Cut", key: "⌘X", enabled: false }),
            ({ id: "copy", label: "Copy", key: "⌘C", enabled: false }),
            ({ id: "paste", label: "Paste", key: "⌘V", enabled: false }),
            ({ divider: true }),
            ({ id: "selall", label: "Select All", key: "⌘A", enabled: false }),
        ] }),
        ({ id: "window", label: "Window", items: this.windowItems(this.winSeq, app.frontWin) }),
        ({ id: "help", label: "Help", items: [
            ({ id: "hsearch", kind: "HelpSearch" }),
            ({ id: "help-app", label: this.nameOf(this.activeApp) + " Help", icon: "lightbulb" }),
        ] }),
    ] },

    // The application menu: About, View & Edit Source, and — except for Files,
    // which is always running — Quit.

    appMenuItems(a) {
        const nm = this.nameOf(a)
        const out = []
        out.push(({ id: "about-app", label: "About " + nm }))
        out.push(({ divider: true }))
        out.push(({ id: "source", label: "View & Edit Source" }))
        if (a != "files") {
            out.push(({ divider: true }))
            out.push(({ id: "quit", label: "Quit " + nm, key: "⌘Q" }))
            }
        return out
        },

    // the Window menu is live state as records: the active application's open
    // windows, front one checked. `seq` and `front` arrive as arguments so
    // the menus constraint above visibly reads them — its re-derive hooks.

    isPlain(w) { return w != null && w.plain == true },      // any-typed seams for a View-typed list
    titleOf(w) { return w != null && w.title ? "" + w.title : "" },

    windowItems(seq, front) {
        const a = this.activeApp
        const ws = this.wins.children
        const out = []
        for (let i = 0; i < ws.length; i++) {
            if (this.isPlain(ws[i]) || this.appOf(ws[i]) != a) continue
            out.push(({ id: "win:" + i, label: this.titleOf(ws[i]) || this.nameOf(a), checked: ws[i] == front }))
            }
        if (out.length == 0) out.push(({ id: "nowin", label: "No Windows", enabled: false }))
        return out
        },

    // No background: the menu bar's words render straight on the wallpaper.
    // `ink` turns the titles light, and the wallpaper's top scrim keeps them
    // legible over any palette.

    bar: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { app.width }, height = { app.menuBarH },
        menuInk: Color = 0xF2F2F5,
        mb: MenuBar [ x = 10, height = { parent.height }, menus = { app.menus }, ink = { parent.menuInk },
            picked(id) { app.command(id) },
            ],
        clock: Text [ x = { parent.width - this.width - 14 }, y = center, fontSize = 13,
            fontWeight = medium,                 // 400 reads thin under browser antialiasing; medium holds up
            textColor = { parent.menuInk }, text = { app.clockText } ],
        ],

segments.md:
### The dock

A translucent plate holding the icon row, the minimized-window tiles, and the
magnification swell (see "The dock's magnification swell" above). Its width is
the plate's, and it stays centered at the bottom of the screen.

segments.html:
dock: View [ height = 150,
        width = { this.plate.width },
        x = { (app.width - this.width) / 2 },
        y = { app.height - 155.5 },           // the 88px plate sits 5.5px off the bottom edge

        // The enter/exit envelope the icons scale by, sprung fast (~100ms), so
        // entry reads as one quick motion; inside the strip the icons track the
        // pointer directly. The band has hysteresis: it arms a fixed distance
        // above the bottom edge but, while hot, holds higher — past a magnified
        // icon's crest — so hovering the top of a grown icon doesn't fall out of
        // the band and collapse the dock under the pointer.
        //
        // `hovering` gates the whole band: when the pointer leaves the browser
        // window it clears, so the dock cools instead of staying magnified at
        // the pointer's last known column.

        armBand: number = 100,                   // the hover band arms this far above the bottom edge (~plate top at 93.5)…
        holdBand: number = 132,                  // …and holds to here while hot (past a magnified icon's crest ~104)
        hot: number = 0,

        // hotGate is the INSTANT band result (0/1). The app icons read it as
        // their wave gate, so their own per-icon mag springs do all the enter/
        // leave smoothing (no double-spring). `hot` is the SPRUNG version — the
        // mini section (a shared pointer-spring layout) still eases through it.

        hotGate: number = { app.hovering && app.pointerY > app.height - (this.hot > 0.5 ? this.holdBand : this.armBand) ? 1 : 0 },

        heat: Spring [ attribute = hot, to = { parent.hotGate }, stiffness = 3600, damping = 120, epsilon = 0.002 ],

        // at rest the icons sit clearly inside the plate; a magnified icon
        // crests above its top — bottom-anchored, top rises. The row must be
        // dock-height: the icons' bottom anchor reads parent.height, and a
        // shorter row would float them above the plate at rest.

        plate: View [ x = 0, y = { parent.height - 88 }, height = 88, width = { parent.row.width + 32 + app.miniSpan + app.miniMagX },
            cornerRadius = 27, fill = { app.darkMode ? 0x2A2A30 : 0xFCFCFE }, opacity = 0.4 ],

        // The plate's edge rim, DRAWN at full strength (the plate is translucent,
        // so the rim rides a sibling above it, not inside its opacity). A top-lit
        // inner highlight: a vertical white gradient stroke, brightest along the
        // TOP edge and fading down the sides to nothing at the floor — a "lit
        // from above" bevel. A faint outer hairline sets the plate off from the
        // wallpaper.

        plateRim: View [ x = 0, y = { parent.height - 88 }, height = 88, width = { parent.plate.width },
            draw(d) {
                const w = this.width
                const h = this.height
                const r = 27
                d.strokeStyle = app.darkMode ? "rgba(0,0,0,0.34)" : "rgba(112,124,148,0.22)"
                d.lineWidth = 1
                d.beginPath()
                d.roundRect(0.5, 0.5, w - 1, h - 1, r - 0.5)
                d.stroke()
                const g = d.createLinearGradient(0, 0, 0, h)
                g.addColorStop(0, app.darkMode ? "rgba(255,255,255,0.30)" : "rgba(255,255,255,0.62)")
                g.addColorStop(0.42, app.darkMode ? "rgba(255,255,255,0.05)" : "rgba(255,255,255,0.13)")
                g.addColorStop(1, "rgba(255,255,255,0)")
                d.strokeStyle = g
                d.lineWidth = 1.5
                d.beginPath()
                d.roundRect(1.4, 1.4, w - 2.8, h - 2.8, r - 1.4)
                d.stroke()
                },
            ],
        row: View [ x = 16, y = 0, height = { parent.height },
            layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 16 ],

            // a running app's icon raises its open windows instead of opening
            // more — dockOpen: raise if any, else the app's default open
            // (which itself decides launch-bounce vs instant by the flag)

            DockIcon [ ix = 0, name = "Files", kind = "files", hue1 = #59B0F6, hue2 = #1D6FD1,
                running = true,
                onClick() { app.dockOpen("files", () => app.newFiles()) },
                ],
            viewerIcon: DockIcon [ ix = 1, name = "Markdown", glyph = "M↓", hue1 = #8E7CE8, hue2 = #5A46B8,
                running = { app.mdRunning },
                onClick() { app.dockOpen("markdown", () => app.openViewer("../../docs/declare.md", "declare.md")) },
                ],

            // the Declare Viewer as a dock application: launches (bounce, dot,
            // Quit) like the others, hosting the real apps/viewer as an
            // embedded app — opened, by default, on this program's own source

            dvIcon: DockIcon [ ix = 2, name = "Declare Viewer", glyph = "[  ]", gsize = 0.42,
                hue1 = #2E6FE0, hue2 = #12A594,   // the brand's blue→teal accent thread
                running = { app.dvRunning },
                onClick() { app.dockOpen("declareviewer", () => app.openApp("../../viewer/viewer", "Declare Viewer", this, "program=desktop.declare")) },
                ],

            // A plausible set of applications — enough icons for the
            // magnification swell to read well.

            calIcon: DockIcon [ ix = 3, name = "Calendar", kind = "calendar", hue1 = #FFFFFF, hue2 = #EFEFF2,
                running = { app.calRunning },
                onClick() { app.dockOpen("calendar", () => app.openApp("../../calendar/calendar", "Calendar", this, "base=../calendar/")) },
                ],
            jotIcon: DockIcon [ ix = 4, name = "Jots", kind = "jots", hue1 = #FCF4A7, hue2 = #F3E27E,
                running = { app.jotRunning },
                onClick() { app.dockOpen("jots", () => app.newJot("", this)) },
                ],

            // "Write" — an inert placeholder (a pencil), to the right of
            // Jots; does nothing when clicked

            DockIcon [ ix = 5, name = "Write", kind = "pencil", hue1 = #F49B45, hue2 = #C96B1D,
                onClick() { app.notImpl("No.", "Write", "pencil", "", 0.42, 0xF49B45, 0xC96B1D) },
                ],
            DockIcon [ ix = 6, name = "Mail", kind = "mail", hue1 = #1E8CFB, hue2 = #1667D9,
                onClick() { app.notImpl("Nope.", "Mail", "mail", "", 0.42, 0x1E8CFB, 0x1667D9) },
                ],
            DockIcon [ ix = 7, name = "Music", glyph = "♪", gsize = 0.52, hue1 = #FB5C74, hue2 = #FA233B,
                onClick() { app.notImpl("Nah.", "Music", "", "♪", 0.52, 0xFB5C74, 0xFA233B) },
                ],
            DockIcon [ ix = 8, name = "Terminal", kind = "terminal", hue1 = #3A3A40, hue2 = #17171B,
                onClick() { app.notImpl("Not.", "Terminal", "terminal", "", 0.42, 0x3A3A40, 0x17171B) },
                ],
            ],

        // ── The minimized-window section: a vertical divider, then a hit tile
        // per minimized window — the visuals are the parked windows themselves,
        // riding the same swell. The section exists only while the sprung span
        // is open, and the divider fades in with the widening, so growth reads as
        // one motion with no seam.

        minis: View [ visible = { app.miniSpan > 0.5 },
            x = { parent.row.x + parent.row.width + 16 }, y = 0, height = { parent.height },
            div: View [ x = 0, y = { parent.height - 77 }, width = 1, height = 65,
                opacity = { Math.min(1, app.miniSpan / 26) },
                fill = { app.darkMode ? 0xFFFFFF48 : 0x00000042 } ],

            // a static pool of tiles, not replication: each is a pure
            // constraint reader over the app's mini state, positioned from
            // the shared layout — there is no per-row machinery to go stale

            rowM: View [ x = 0, y = 0, height = { parent.height },
                MiniTile [ ix = 0 ],
                MiniTile [ ix = 1 ],
                MiniTile [ ix = 2 ],
                MiniTile [ ix = 3 ],
                MiniTile [ ix = 4 ],
                MiniTile [ ix = 5 ],
                ],
            ],
        ],

    // ── The "Not implemented" alert: a translucent app-modal sheet — the scrim
    // swallows every press — sitting frontmost over all the chrome. Its icon
    // reuses the very renderer the dock draws (app.drawKindArt), badged with a
    // caution triangle.

    alert: View [ visible = { app.alertName != "" },
        x = 0, y = 0, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height },
        onMouseDown() { },                           // modal: presses on the scrim do nothing

        scrim: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height }, fill = #00000026 ],

        panel: View [ width = 300, height = 422,
            // The panel sits slightly above center — the top gap is about a
            // third of the empty vertical space, never dead-center.

            x = { (parent.width - this.width) / 2 }, y = { (parent.height - this.height) / 3 },
            cornerRadius = 20,
            fill = { theme.menuMaterial != null ? theme.menuMaterial : theme.surface },
            stroke = stroke(1, #FFFFFF80),
            shadow = shadow(0, 16, 50, #00000059),

            icon: AppGlyph [ x = 118, y = 26, edge = 64,
                kind = { app.alertKind }, glyph = { app.alertGlyph }, gsize = { app.alertGsize },
                hue1 = { app.alertHue1 }, hue2 = { app.alertHue2 } ],
            badge: CautionBadge [ x = 162, y = 70, edge = 30 ],

            title: Text [ x = center, y = 112, fontSize = 15, fontWeight = semibold,
                textColor = { theme.text }, text = { app.alertHead } ],
            msg: Text [ x = 20, y = 136, width = 260, wrap = true, textAlign = center,
                fontSize = 12, textColor = { theme.textMuted },
                text = { "Declare Desktop doesn't include " + app.alertName + "." } ],

            // The payoff — a rounded image with a thin hairline, so it reads as
            // deliberate rather than a stray asset.

            rick: Image [ source = "rick.jpeg", x = 22, y = 184, width = 256, height = 160,
                stretches = both, cornerRadius = 10, clip = true,
                stroke = { stroke(1, app.darkMode ? 0xFFFFFF2E : 0x0000002E) } ],

            ok: Button [ x = 16, y = { parent.height - 46 }, width = { parent.width - 32 },
                label = "OK", primary = true,
                onClick() { app.dismissAlert() },
                ],
            ],
        ],
    ]
